api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.AbstractWatermar.title: Class AbstractWatermarkValue | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.AbstractWatermar.summary: Class AbstractWatermarkValue Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Base abstract class for watermark values used in the dataflow stream to track data progression. public abstract class AbstractWatermarkValue : IComparable<AbstractWatermarkValue> Inheritance object AbstractWatermarkValue Implements IComparable<AbstractWatermarkValue> Derived AbstractWatermarkValue<T> Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This class provides a common structure for all watermark values, including a BatchID that is used to split up a watermark with the same value into multiple batches. Properties BatchID Gets or sets the batch identifier associated with this watermark. public long BatchID { get; set; } Property Value long Remarks The BatchID is utilized when a single watermark duration or metric needs to be processed in chunks. By incrementing this ID, the engine splits a primary watermark value into multiple, ordered sub-batches. TypeId Gets the unique identifier for the specific type of watermark. public abstract int TypeId { get; } Property Value int Remarks Used internally to differentiate between various implementations of watermarks during comparisons. Ensures that operations such as equating and sorting only occur between watermarks of the exact same type. Methods Compare(AbstractWatermarkValue?) Compares the internal value of the watermark with another abstract watermark value. public abstract int Compare(AbstractWatermarkValue? other) Parameters other AbstractWatermarkValue The other watermark to compare. Returns int An integer indicating sort order. Remarks This base implementation ignores BatchID. The full comparable sequence is evaluated in CompareTo(AbstractWatermarkValue?). CompareTo(AbstractWatermarkValue?) Compares the current instance with another watermark and returns an integer that indicates their relative position in the sort order. public int CompareTo(AbstractWatermarkValue? other) Parameters other AbstractWatermarkValue An abstract watermark to compare with this instance. Returns int A value that indicates the relative order of the objects being compared. Remarks The comparison first checks the core watermark value using Compare(AbstractWatermarkValue?). If the values are equal, the tie is broken by comparing the BatchID properties. Equals(object?) Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current watermark value. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with the current object. Returns bool true if the specified object is equal to the current object; otherwise, false. GetHashCode() Base hash function of a watermark value, only looks at typeId and batchId. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int A hash code for the current object. Remarks The base implementation combines TypeId and BatchID, which may result in collisions if there are many unique values. Implementors of the abstraction should provide their own hash code logic for fewer collisions. Min(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Returns the smaller of two watermark values. public static AbstractWatermarkValue? Min(AbstractWatermarkValue? a, AbstractWatermarkValue? b) Parameters a AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark value. b AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark value. Returns AbstractWatermarkValue The smaller of the two watermarks, or null if either is null. Operators operator ==(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Determines whether two specified watermark values have the same value. public static bool operator ==(AbstractWatermarkValue? left, AbstractWatermarkValue? right) Parameters left AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark to compare. right AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark to compare. Returns bool true if the value of left is the same as the value of right; otherwise, false. operator >(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Determines whether one specified watermark value is greater than another specified watermark value. public static bool operator >(AbstractWatermarkValue? left, AbstractWatermarkValue? right) Parameters left AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark to compare. right AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark to compare. Returns bool true if left is strictly greater than right; otherwise, false. operator >=(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Determines whether one specified watermark value is greater than or equal to another specified watermark value. public static bool operator >=(AbstractWatermarkValue? left, AbstractWatermarkValue? right) Parameters left AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark to compare. right AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark to compare. Returns bool true if left is greater than or equal to right; otherwise, false. operator !=(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Determines whether two specified watermark values have different values. public static bool operator !=(AbstractWatermarkValue? left, AbstractWatermarkValue? right) Parameters left AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark to compare. right AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark to compare. Returns bool true if the value of left is different from the value of right; otherwise, false. operator <(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Determines whether one specified watermark value is less than another specified watermark value. public static bool operator <(AbstractWatermarkValue? left, AbstractWatermarkValue? right) Parameters left AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark to compare. right AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark to compare. Returns bool true if left is strictly less than right; otherwise, false. operator <=(AbstractWatermarkValue?, AbstractWatermarkValue?) Determines whether one specified watermark value is less than or equal to another specified watermark value. public static bool operator <=(AbstractWatermarkValue? left, AbstractWatermarkValue? right) Parameters left AbstractWatermarkValue The first watermark to compare. right AbstractWatermarkValue The second watermark to compare. Returns bool true if left is less than or equal to right; otherwise, false.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Checkpoint.html.summary: Class Checkpoint Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents a checkpoint event within the dataflow stream. public class Checkpoint : ICheckpointEvent, ILockingEvent, IStreamEvent Inheritance object Checkpoint Implements ICheckpointEvent ILockingEvent IStreamEvent Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Checkpoint events are propagated through the stream to coordinate state persistence and synchronization across different vertices and operators. This ensures fault tolerance and data consistency. api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.CheckFail.title: Struct CheckFailureNotification | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.CheckFail.summary: Struct CheckFailureNotification Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A stack-allocated notification carrying data quality check failure information delivered to ICheckFailureListener implementations. public ref struct CheckFailureNotification Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks This type is a ref struct to avoid heap allocation on each check failure and to allow its Tags field to hold a ReadOnlySpan<T>, which itself cannot appear as a field in a non-ref-struct type. It must not be stored or accessed beyond the duration of the OnCheckFailure(ref readonly CheckFailureNotification) call in which it is received; copy any required fields to local variables if they must outlive the call. Instances are created internally by StreamNotificationReceiver each time a compiled SQL CHECK expression calls OnCheckFailure(in string, ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>>) with a failing message and diagnostic tags. Constructors CheckFailureNotification(ref string, string, ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>>) Initializes a new CheckFailureNotification with a reference to the stream name, the failure message, and the associated diagnostic tags. public CheckFailureNotification(ref string streamName, string message, ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>> tags) Parameters streamName string A ref to the stream name string held by StreamNotificationReceiver. message string The human-readable failure message from the SQL CHECK expression. tags ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object>> A stack-allocated span of key-value diagnostic tag pairs from the CHECK expression. Must not be stored beyond the scope of the constructor call. Fields Message The human-readable failure message declared in the SQL CHECK expression. public readonly string Message Field Value string StreamName A read-only managed reference to the name of the stream in which the check failed. public readonly ref string StreamName Field Value string Tags A stack-allocated span of named diagnostic key-value pairs declared as tag arguments in the SQL CHECK expression. Must not be stored or accessed after the enclosing OnCheckFailure(ref readonly CheckFailureNotification) call returns. public readonly ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>> Tags Field Value ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object>>

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DataflowS.title: Class DataflowStream | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DataflowS.summary: Class DataflowStream Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll The primary public-facing handle for a Flowtide dataflow stream, providing lifecycle management, monitoring, and operational control over the stream. public class DataflowStream : IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object DataflowStream Implements IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks A DataflowStream wraps an internal StreamContext state machine and exposes an API for starting, stopping, pausing, checkpointing, and inspecting the stream. Instances are created via Build() and are not intended to be constructed directly. The stream can be driven in two ways depending on the configured IStreamScheduler: Call RunAsync() to start the stream and block the caller within a scheduler tick loop. This is the typical usage when using the DefaultStreamScheduler, and is how the hosted service StreamWorkerService operates it. Call StartAsync() to initiate the startup sequence only, leaving scheduler tick management to the caller. This is appropriate for custom IStreamScheduler implementations. The stream progresses through StreamStateValue states (e.g. NotStarted, Starting, Running, Failure) and StreamStatus operational statuses (e.g. Running, Paused, Failing). The combined Health property derives a FlowtideHealth value suitable for health checks and monitoring. Properties Health Gets the overall health of the stream, derived from the combination of State and Status. public FlowtideHealth Health { get; } Property Value FlowtideHealth Remarks Returns Healthy when the stream is running normally, Degraded during start-up or when paused, and Unhealthy when the stream has faulted or stopped. Suitable for use with health check integrations. Scheduler Gets the IStreamScheduler instance responsible for scheduling and firing registered trigger events within this stream. public IStreamScheduler Scheduler { get; } Property Value IStreamScheduler State Gets the current high-level state machine value of the stream. public StreamStateValue State { get; } Property Value StreamStateValue Remarks Reflects the current position within the stream lifecycle, such as NotStarted, Running, Failure, or Stopping. Status Gets the current operational status of the stream. public StreamStatus Status { get; } Property Value StreamStatus Remarks Provides finer-grained operational information than State, including values such as Running, Paused, Degraded, and Failing. WantedState Gets the desired target state that the stream is transitioning towards. public StreamStateValue WantedState { get; } Property Value StreamStateValue Remarks During a state transition the current State may differ from WantedState until the transition completes. Methods CallTrigger(string, object?) Manually invokes a named trigger on all operators registered to handle it. public Task CallTrigger(string name, object? state) Parameters name string The name of the trigger to invoke. state object Optional state object passed to the trigger handler. Returns Task A task that completes when all trigger handlers have been queued. DeleteAsync() Transitions the stream to the deleting state, permanently removing all persistent state associated with every vertex in the stream. public Task DeleteAsync() Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous delete operation. DisposeAsync() Releases all resources held by the stream asynchronously, completing all dataflow blocks and disposing each vertex. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A ValueTask representing the asynchronous dispose operation. GetDiagnosticsGraph() Returns a StreamGraph describing the current operator topology of the stream, including per-vertex metric snapshots and the directed edges between operators. public StreamGraph GetDiagnosticsGraph() Returns StreamGraph A StreamGraph containing all operator nodes with their current metric counters and gauges, the edges between them, and the current State. GetLatestState() Returns the latest persisted StreamState, or null if no state has been saved yet. public StreamState? GetLatestState() Returns StreamState The most recent StreamState loaded or saved by the stream, or null if the stream has not yet completed its first checkpoint. Pause() Suspends message processing throughout the stream. Data will continue to queue but will not be processed until Resume() is called. public void Pause() Remarks Sets the operational Status to Paused for the duration of the pause. Has no effect if the stream is already paused. Resume() Resumes message processing after a prior call to Pause(), restoring the Status to its value before the pause was applied. public void Resume() RunAsync() Starts the stream and drives the scheduler tick loop until the stream transitions to NotStarted. public Task RunAsync() Returns Task A task that completes when the stream stops running. Remarks This method is only active when the configured IStreamScheduler is a DefaultStreamScheduler. It calls StartAsync() internally, then enters a 10 ms periodic timer loop that calls Tick on the scheduler to dispatch any due trigger events. A full garbage collection is performed once every 10 seconds (every 1000 ticks) to manage native memory pressure from large stream workloads. When using a custom IStreamScheduler, use StartAsync() directly instead and manage the scheduler externally. StartAsync() Initiates the stream startup sequence, setting up all blocks, linking them, and restoring state from the last checkpoint if available. public Task StartAsync() Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous startup operation. StopAsync() Gracefully stops the stream, waiting for the stop sequence to complete before returning. public Task StopAsync() Returns Task A task that completes when the stream has fully stopped. TriggerCheckpoint() Triggers a manual checkpoint on the stream, requesting all vertices to persist their current state. public Task TriggerCheckpoint() Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous checkpoint trigger operation. TryScheduleCheckpoint(TimeSpan) Schedules a checkpoint to be triggered after the specified delay, respecting any configured minimum time between checkpoints. public void TryScheduleCheckpoint(TimeSpan timeSpan) Parameters timeSpan TimeSpan The delay after which the checkpoint should be triggered.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DataflowS.title: Class DataflowStreamBuilder | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DataflowS.summary: Class DataflowStreamBuilder Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A fluent builder for configuring and constructing a DataflowStream. public class DataflowStreamBuilder Inheritance object DataflowStreamBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks DataflowStreamBuilder assembles all components required to run a dataflow stream: ingress vertices (sources), propagator vertices (transforms), and egress vertices (sinks), together with state management, scheduling, logging, versioning, and notification callbacks. Each configuration method returns the same builder instance to support method chaining. The only required configuration before calling Build() is WithStateOptions(StateManagerOptions), which provides the state manager persistence settings. All other configuration is optional and falls back to sensible defaults: WithStreamScheduler(IStreamScheduler) defaults to DefaultStreamScheduler. WithStateHandler(IStateHandler) defaults to a no-op NullStateHandler. For higher-level SQL-plan-based stream construction, prefer FlowtideBuilder from FlowtideDotNet.Core, which wraps this builder and handles plan compilation, operator wiring, and hash-based version tracking automatically. Constructors DataflowStreamBuilder(string) Initializes a new instance of DataflowStreamBuilder for the stream with the given name. public DataflowStreamBuilder(string streamName) Parameters streamName string The unique name of the stream. Used to namespace state storage, metrics, and log output throughout the lifetime of the stream. Methods AddCheckFailureListener(ICheckFailureListener) Subscribes an ICheckFailureListener to receive a notification each time a data quality check within the stream fails. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddCheckFailureListener(ICheckFailureListener listener) Parameters listener ICheckFailureListener The listener to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Multiple listeners can be registered. Exceptions thrown by a listener are swallowed to ensure they cannot disrupt the stream. AddCheckpointListener(ICheckpointListener) Subscribes an ICheckpointListener to receive a notification each time the stream completes a checkpoint boundary. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddCheckpointListener(ICheckpointListener listener) Parameters listener ICheckpointListener The listener to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Multiple listeners can be registered. Exceptions thrown by a listener are swallowed to ensure they cannot disrupt the stream. AddEgressBlock(string, IStreamEgressVertex) Registers an egress (sink) vertex under the given operator name. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddEgressBlock(string name, IStreamEgressVertex block) Parameters name string The unique operator name for this vertex within the stream. block IStreamEgressVertex The egress vertex to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Egress vertices are the terminal points of the stream, consuming IStreamEvent messages and writing results to external systems. Setup(string, string) is called on the block immediately. AddFailureListener(IFailureListener) Subscribes an IFailureListener to receive a notification each time the stream encounters an unhandled exception and transitions to a failure state. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddFailureListener(IFailureListener listener) Parameters listener IFailureListener The listener to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Multiple listeners can be registered. Exceptions thrown by a listener are swallowed to ensure they cannot disrupt the stream. AddIngressBlock(string, IStreamIngressVertex) Registers an ingress (source) vertex under the given operator name. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddIngressBlock(string name, IStreamIngressVertex block) Parameters name string The unique operator name for this vertex within the stream. block IStreamIngressVertex The ingress vertex to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Ingress vertices are the entry points of the stream, producing IStreamEvent messages from external data sources. Setup(string, string) is called on the block immediately. AddPropagatorBlock(string, IStreamVertex) Registers a propagator (transform) vertex under the given operator name. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddPropagatorBlock(string name, IStreamVertex block) Parameters name string The unique operator name for this vertex within the stream. block IStreamVertex The propagator vertex to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Propagator vertices sit between ingress and egress vertices, transforming or routing stream events. The name is used as the operator's unique identity for state storage, metrics, and inter-vertex linking. Setup(string, string) is called on the block immediately so it is ready for linking. AddStateChangeListener(IStreamStateChangeListener) Subscribes an IStreamStateChangeListener to receive a notification each time the stream transitions to a new StreamStateValue. public DataflowStreamBuilder AddStateChangeListener(IStreamStateChangeListener listener) Parameters listener IStreamStateChangeListener The listener to register. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Multiple listeners can be registered. Exceptions thrown by a listener are swallowed to ensure they cannot disrupt the stream. Build() Validates the configuration, applies defaults for any unset optional components, and constructs the fully configured DataflowStream. public DataflowStream Build() Returns DataflowStream A fully configured but not yet started DataflowStream. Remarks If WithStateHandler(IStateHandler) was not called, a no-op NullStateHandler is used. If WithStreamScheduler(IStreamScheduler) was not called, a DefaultStreamScheduler is created. The returned DataflowStream is not yet started. Call StartAsync() or RunAsync() to begin processing. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if WithStateOptions(StateManagerOptions) has not been called before Build(). FromState(StreamState) Seeds the builder with a previously persisted StreamState to restore the stream from a known logical time rather than starting fresh. public DataflowStreamBuilder FromState(StreamState state) Parameters state StreamState The stream state to restore from. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. SetMinimumTimeBetweenCheckpoint(TimeSpan) Sets the minimum time that must elapse between two consecutive checkpoint triggers, preventing excessive checkpointing under high-throughput conditions. public DataflowStreamBuilder SetMinimumTimeBetweenCheckpoint(TimeSpan timeSpan) Parameters timeSpan TimeSpan The minimum interval between checkpoints. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. SetStopDrainTimeout(TimeSpan) Sets how long a stopping stream waits for vertices that exchange data with other substreams to drain before it finishes stopping anyway. Protects the stop from waiting forever when another substream has crashed or never started. public DataflowStreamBuilder SetStopDrainTimeout(TimeSpan timeSpan) Parameters timeSpan TimeSpan The maximum time to wait for the drain. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. SetVersion(string) Sets the semantic version string for this stream configuration. public DataflowStreamBuilder SetVersion(string version) Parameters version string A non-null, non-whitespace version string identifying this stream configuration. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks Unlike SetVersionInformation(string, string), this method sets only the human-readable version label without a structural hash. It is stored alongside persisted state for diagnostic and upgrade tracking purposes. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown if version is null or whitespace. SetVersionInformation(string, string) Sets the stream's plan hash and human-readable version string, used to detect structural changes between stream restarts and guard against incompatible state restores. public DataflowStreamBuilder SetVersionInformation(string hash, string version) Parameters hash string A content hash of the stream plan, used for structural change detection. version string A human-readable version label associated with this stream configuration. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks When set, the stream engine compares hash against the hash stored in persisted state on startup. A mismatch causes an InvalidOperationException to be thrown, preventing accidental state corruption when the stream topology has changed. WaitForCheckpointAfterInitialData(bool) Controls whether the stream should wait for a checkpoint to complete after all ingress vertices have finished emitting their initial data load. public DataflowStreamBuilder WaitForCheckpointAfterInitialData(bool wait) Parameters wait bool true to wait for a checkpoint after initial data; false to proceed immediately. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks When true (the default), the stream takes a checkpoint immediately after initial data is fully propagated, establishing a clean recovery baseline before transitioning to incremental processing. WithLoggerFactory(ILoggerFactory) Sets the ILoggerFactory used to create loggers for all vertices and internal stream components. public DataflowStreamBuilder WithLoggerFactory(ILoggerFactory loggerFactory) Parameters loggerFactory ILoggerFactory The logger factory to use. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. WithPauseMonitor(IOptionsMonitor<FlowtidePauseOptions>) Wires up an IOptionsMonitor<TOptions> so that the stream can be dynamically paused and resumed at runtime through configuration changes without restarting. public DataflowStreamBuilder WithPauseMonitor(IOptionsMonitor<FlowtidePauseOptions> pauseMonitor) Parameters pauseMonitor IOptionsMonitor<FlowtidePauseOptions> The options monitor to observe for pause/resume state changes. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. Remarks If IsPaused is already true when Build() is called, the stream starts in a paused state. Subsequent changes to the options monitor are applied immediately via the monitor's change callback. WithStateHandler(IStateHandler) Sets the IStateHandler responsible for persisting and loading top-level stream state snapshots. Defaults to a no-op handler if not specified. public DataflowStreamBuilder WithStateHandler(IStateHandler stateHandler) Parameters stateHandler IStateHandler The state handler to use. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. WithStateOptions(StateManagerOptions) Sets the StateManagerOptions that configure how operator state is persisted and managed. This is the only required configuration before calling Build(). public DataflowStreamBuilder WithStateOptions(StateManagerOptions stateManagerOptions) Parameters stateManagerOptions StateManagerOptions The persistence options for the state manager. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining. WithStreamScheduler(IStreamScheduler) Replaces the default DefaultStreamScheduler with a custom IStreamScheduler implementation for managing trigger scheduling. public DataflowStreamBuilder WithStreamScheduler(IStreamScheduler streamScheduler) Parameters streamScheduler IStreamScheduler The custom scheduler to use. Returns DataflowStreamBuilder This builder instance for method chaining.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DefaultSt.title: Class DefaultStreamScheduler | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DefaultSt.summary: Class DefaultStreamScheduler Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll The default IStreamScheduler implementation that dispatches trigger events to stream operators at configurable recurring intervals. public class DefaultStreamScheduler : IStreamScheduler Inheritance object DefaultStreamScheduler Implements IStreamScheduler Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Scheduling is backed by a min-heap priority queue keyed on the next UTC fire time, together with a dictionary that maps each {operatorName}.{triggerName} key to its recurrence interval. When Schedule(string, string, TimeSpan) is called, the trigger's first fire time is set to DateTime.UtcNow + interval and the entry is enqueued. On each Tick() call, all entries whose fire time is in the past are dequeued, re-enqueued at the next interval, and dispatched via the IStreamTriggerCaller supplied at Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller). Tick() is not called automatically; it must be driven externally. When using RunAsync(), the stream's internal 10 ms periodic loop calls Tick() on every iteration. Custom IStreamScheduler implementations or hosting scenarios that call StartAsync() directly are responsible for driving Tick() at the desired resolution. All public members are declared virtual so that derived classes can override individual scheduling behaviours without replacing the entire implementation. Constructors DefaultStreamScheduler() Initializes a new instance of DefaultStreamScheduler with an empty schedule. public DefaultStreamScheduler() Methods Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller) Connects this scheduler to the stream engine, supplying the caller used to dispatch trigger events when their scheduled time arrives. public virtual void Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller streamTriggerCaller) Parameters streamTriggerCaller IStreamTriggerCaller The IStreamTriggerCaller provided by the stream engine that routes trigger events to the appropriate operator vertices. Remarks Called automatically by StreamContext immediately after the scheduler is assigned to the stream. Must be called before Tick() can dispatch any triggers. RemoveSchedule(string, string) Removes a previously registered trigger schedule so that it is no longer dispatched. public virtual Task RemoveSchedule(string triggerName, string operatorName) Parameters triggerName string The name of the trigger to remove. operatorName string The name of the operator that owns the trigger. Returns Task A completed task. Remarks Only the interval dictionary entry is removed. Any pending entry in the priority queue is left in place and will be silently dropped the next time it is dequeued during Tick(). Schedule(string, string, TimeSpan) Registers a recurring trigger that will be dispatched at the specified interval. public virtual Task Schedule(string triggerName, string operatorName, TimeSpan interval) Parameters triggerName string The name identifying the trigger within the operator. operatorName string The name of the operator that owns the trigger. interval TimeSpan The recurring interval at which the trigger should fire. Returns Task A completed task. Remarks The trigger is enqueued immediately with a first fire time of DateTime.UtcNow + interval. On each subsequent fire, it is automatically re-enqueued at DateTime.UtcNow + interval from the moment it was dispatched. The combined key {operatorName}.{triggerName} must be unique; registering a duplicate key will throw a ArgumentException from the underlying dictionary. Tick() Checks the schedule queue and dispatches all triggers whose fire time has passed. public virtual Task Tick() Returns Task A task that completes when all overdue triggers for this tick have been dispatched. Remarks Each overdue trigger is dequeued, re-enqueued at DateTime.UtcNow + interval to schedule its next recurrence, and then dispatched via CallTrigger(string, object?). If the trigger has been removed via RemoveSchedule(string, string) since it was enqueued, it is silently discarded without being dispatched. The lock is released around each CallTrigger invocation so that scheduling operations from other threads can proceed while the trigger handler executes. This method must be called externally to advance the scheduler. When using RunAsync(), it is called automatically inside the 10 ms timer loop.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.ICheckFai.title: Interface ICheckFailureListener | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.ICheckFai.summary: Interface ICheckFailureListener Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines a receiver for data quality check failure notifications raised by the stream. public interface ICheckFailureListener Remarks Implementations are registered on a DataflowStreamBuilder via AddCheckFailureListener(ICheckFailureListener) and are invoked each time a SQL-level CHECK assertion evaluated within the stream produces a failing result. Multiple listeners can be registered; each is called in registration order. The notification is passed as a ref readonly CheckFailureNotification, which is a ref struct. This avoids a heap allocation and allows the notification to carry a ReadOnlySpan<T> of diagnostic tags. Implementations must therefore not store the notification beyond the duration of OnCheckFailure(ref readonly CheckFailureNotification) — copy any required fields to local variables instead. Exceptions thrown by an implementation are swallowed by the stream engine to ensure that a misbehaving listener cannot disrupt stream processing. The built-in implementations are LoggerCheckFailureListener, which forwards the failure message and tags to ILogger, and ActivityCheckFailureListener, which emits an OpenTelemetry Activity under the source FlowtideDotNet.CheckFailures. Methods OnCheckFailure(ref readonly CheckFailureNotification) Called when a data quality check within the stream produces a failing result. void OnCheckFailure(ref readonly CheckFailureNotification notification) Parameters notification CheckFailureNotification A ref readonly CheckFailureNotification carrying the stream name, failure message, and a ReadOnlySpan<T> of diagnostic tags that describe the context in which the check failed. Remarks The notification is a ref readonly ref struct; it must not be stored or captured beyond the scope of this method call. Access StreamName to identify the originating stream, Message for the human-readable failure description, and Tags for the associated diagnostic key-value metadata.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.ICheckNot.title: Interface ICheckNotificationReceiver | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.ICheckNot.summary: Interface ICheckNotificationReceiver Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines the internal compute-layer sink for data quality check failure events raised directly from compiled SQL CHECK expressions. public interface ICheckNotificationReceiver Remarks This interface is the low-level counterpart to ICheckFailureListener. Where ICheckFailureListener is the user-facing observer registered on DataflowStreamBuilder, ICheckNotificationReceiver is the compute-layer target whose reference is baked into the compiled expression tree of every SQL CHECK function at stream build time. When a CHECK condition evaluates to false for a row, the generated expression calls OnCheckFailure(in string, ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>>) directly via a constant reference captured in the expression, making this a hot-path call that may occur for every failing row during stream processing. The sole built-in implementation is StreamNotificationReceiver, which bridges this compute-layer interface to the set of registered ICheckFailureListener instances by wrapping the arguments in a stack-allocated CheckFailureNotification ref struct and dispatching it to each listener in turn. The receiver is wired up during stream initialization through FunctionsRegister.SetCheckNotificationReceiver, which stores it in FunctionServices so that the expression compiler can retrieve it when building CHECK function expressions. Methods OnCheckFailure(in string, ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>>) Called from within a compiled SQL CHECK expression when its condition evaluates to false for a row being processed. void OnCheckFailure(in string message, ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object?>> tags) Parameters message string The human-readable failure message declared in the SQL CHECK expression, passed by read-only reference to avoid an additional pointer copy on the call stack. tags ReadOnlySpan<KeyValuePair<string, object>> A stack-allocated ReadOnlySpan<T> of KeyValuePair<TKey, TValue> entries providing named diagnostic context values declared as tag arguments in the SQL CHECK expression. Must not be stored or accessed after this method returns. Remarks This method is invoked on the hot data path and may be called for every row in a stream batch where the check fails. Implementations should be allocation-free and return as quickly as possible to avoid stalling stream throughput. The message parameter uses the in modifier so the string reference is passed by read-only reference without copying the pointer. The tags span is stack-allocated by the compiled expression and must not be stored or accessed after this method returns.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.ICheckpoi.title: Interface ICheckpointListener | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.ICheckpoi.summary: Interface ICheckpointListener Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines a receiver for checkpoint completion notifications raised by the stream. public interface ICheckpointListener Remarks Implementations are registered on a DataflowStreamBuilder via AddCheckpointListener(ICheckpointListener) and are invoked each time the stream successfully completes a full checkpoint boundary. Multiple listeners can be registered; each is called in registration order. OnCheckpointComplete(StreamCheckpointNotification) is called after all egress vertices have signalled their checkpoint is done and after the state manager has durably written the checkpoint to persistent storage, but before ingress vertices receive their CheckpointDone callback. This makes it the correct integration point for any external system that must be notified only once stream state is safely committed — for example to advance a consumer offset, trigger a dependent workflow, or record a checkpoint metric. Exceptions thrown by an implementation are swallowed by the stream engine to ensure that a misbehaving listener cannot disrupt stream processing or block the checkpoint sequence. Methods OnCheckpointComplete(StreamCheckpointNotification) Called when the stream has successfully completed a full checkpoint and durably written all operator state to persistent storage. void OnCheckpointComplete(StreamCheckpointNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamCheckpointNotification A StreamCheckpointNotification carrying the name of the stream that completed the checkpoint boundary. Remarks This method is invoked synchronously within the checkpoint completion task. Implementations should avoid long-running or blocking operations; if coordination with an external system is required, consider starting a background task rather than blocking the checkpoint completion path. Access StreamName to identify the stream that completed the checkpoint.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IFailureL.title: Interface IFailureListener | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IFailureL.summary: Interface IFailureListener Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines a receiver for stream failure notifications raised when an unhandled exception causes the stream to transition to a failure state. public interface IFailureListener Remarks Implementations are registered on a DataflowStreamBuilder via AddFailureListener(IFailureListener) and are invoked each time the stream encounters an unhandled exception that triggers a failure state transition. Multiple listeners can be registered; each is called in registration order. OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) is called before the stream begins its automatic recovery sequence. After dispatching to all listeners, the stream stops and disposes its running blocks, then automatically restarts from the last successfully committed checkpoint. Listeners can use this notification to implement custom recovery strategies — for example ExitProcessStrategy calls Exit(int) to let a container orchestrator handle the restart, and KillProcessStrategy forcibly kills the process via Kill(). Use FlowtideBuilder.WithFailureListener to register a custom Action<T> callback without implementing the full interface. Exceptions thrown by an implementation are swallowed by the stream engine to ensure that a misbehaving listener cannot prevent the failure state from being entered or the automatic recovery from proceeding. Methods OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) Called when the stream encounters an unhandled exception and is about to enter a failure state and initiate its automatic recovery sequence. void OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamFailureNotification A StreamFailureNotification carrying the name of the stream that failed and the optional exception that caused the failure. Remarks This method is invoked synchronously within the failure transition. Implementations should avoid long-running or blocking operations, as this call occurs before the stream has stopped its running blocks. Access StreamName to identify the affected stream, and Exception for the cause of the failure, which may be null in cases where no specific exception was captured.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStateHan.title: Interface IStateHandler | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStateHan.summary: Interface IStateHandler Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll public interface IStateHandler Methods LoadLatestState(string) Task<StreamState?> LoadLatestState(string stateName) Parameters stateName string Returns Task<StreamState>

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStreamSc.title: Interface IStreamScheduler | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStreamSc.summary: Interface IStreamScheduler Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines the contract for scheduling and dispatching recurring trigger events to stream operators. public interface IStreamScheduler Remarks An IStreamScheduler is configured on a DataflowStreamBuilder via WithStreamScheduler(IStreamScheduler). If no scheduler is provided, DefaultStreamScheduler is used automatically. The stream engine calls Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller) once during StreamContext construction, supplying itself as the IStreamTriggerCaller through which trigger events are dispatched to operators. Schedule(string, string, TimeSpan) is called each time a vertex registers a recurring trigger (for example via RegisterTrigger inside a vertex's Initialize override). All registered schedules are cleared between stream restarts by calls to RemoveSchedule(string, string) from StreamContext.ClearTriggers, ensuring that vertices re-register their triggers with the correct interval on every startup. Implementations are free to use any timing mechanism — for example a background Timer, a hosted service, or Quartz.NET — as long as they ultimately call CallTrigger(string, object?) when a trigger is due. DefaultStreamScheduler uses a priority-queue approach driven externally by RunAsync()'s 10 ms periodic tick loop. Methods Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller) Connects this scheduler to the stream engine and prepares it to begin dispatching triggers. void Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller streamTriggerCaller) Parameters streamTriggerCaller IStreamTriggerCaller The IStreamTriggerCaller provided by the stream engine that routes a named trigger event to all operator vertices that registered it. Remarks Called once by StreamContext immediately after the scheduler is assigned, before any calls to Schedule(string, string, TimeSpan). The supplied streamTriggerCaller must be stored and used by subsequent scheduling operations to dispatch trigger events to the correct operator vertices. RemoveSchedule(string, string) Removes a previously registered trigger schedule so that it is no longer dispatched. Task RemoveSchedule(string triggerName, string operatorName) Parameters triggerName string The name of the trigger to remove. operatorName string The name of the operator that owns the trigger. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous removal operation. Remarks Called by StreamContext.ClearTriggers before each stream restart to reset all active schedules. Vertices re-register their triggers on every startup, so implementations must honour this removal and stop dispatching the given trigger after this call returns. Schedule(string, string, TimeSpan) Registers a recurring trigger that the scheduler should dispatch at the specified interval. Task Schedule(string triggerName, string operatorName, TimeSpan interval) Parameters triggerName string The name identifying the trigger within the operator. operatorName string The name of the operator that owns the trigger. interval TimeSpan The recurring interval at which the trigger should fire. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous registration operation. Remarks Called by the stream engine each time a vertex registers a periodic trigger during its Initialize phase. The combination of operatorName and triggerName uniquely identifies the schedule entry. Implementations must ensure that CallTrigger(string, object?) is invoked with the same operatorName and triggerName each time the interval elapses.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStreamSt.title: Interface IStreamStateChangeListener | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStreamSt.summary: Interface IStreamStateChangeListener Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines a receiver for state change notifications raised each time the stream transitions to a new StreamStateValue. public interface IStreamStateChangeListener Remarks Implementations are registered on a DataflowStreamBuilder via AddStateChangeListener(IStreamStateChangeListener) and are invoked each time the stream enters a new lifecycle state. Multiple listeners can be registered; each is called in registration order. OnStreamStateChange(StreamStateChangeNotification) is called synchronously within StreamContext.TransitionTo before the state machine transition begins. This means the notification arrives at the moment the new StreamStateValue is recorded — for example Starting, Running, Failure, or Stopping — before any of the associated transition work, such as stopping blocks or re-initializing the stream, is performed. Typical uses include updating an external health dashboard, logging state transitions, or signalling a dependent service. Exceptions thrown by an implementation are swallowed by the stream engine to ensure that a misbehaving listener cannot block a state transition or disrupt stream processing. Methods OnStreamStateChange(StreamStateChangeNotification) Called when the stream transitions to a new StreamStateValue. void OnStreamStateChange(StreamStateChangeNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamStateChangeNotification A StreamStateChangeNotification carrying the name of the stream and the new StreamStateValue that the stream is entering. Remarks This method is invoked synchronously before the transition work begins; implementations should avoid long-running or blocking operations to prevent delaying the stream's lifecycle progression. Access StreamName to identify the affected stream and State for the StreamStateValue the stream is transitioning into.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStreamTr.title: Interface IStreamTriggerCaller | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.IStreamTr.summary: Interface IStreamTriggerCaller Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Defines the contract for dispatching trigger events to one or all operators within a stream. public interface IStreamTriggerCaller Remarks IStreamTriggerCaller is implemented by the stream engine's StreamContext and is passed to an IStreamScheduler via Initialize(IStreamTriggerCaller) so that the scheduler can dispatch trigger events when a scheduled interval elapses. It is also surfaced publicly on CallTrigger(string, object?) for imperative, on-demand trigger invocations from outside the stream. Two dispatch modes are available. The broadcast overload CallTrigger(string, object?) routes the trigger to every operator that has registered a handler for the given triggerName, making it suitable for stream-wide signals such as a scheduled flush. The targeted overload CallTrigger(string, string, object?) routes the trigger to a single named operator, making it suitable for precise, per-operator control. In both cases, each matching operator receives the trigger via its OnTrigger override by queuing a TriggerEvent onto the operator's internal dataflow block. Methods CallTrigger(string, object?) Dispatches a named trigger to every operator in the stream that has registered a handler for triggerName. Task CallTrigger(string triggerName, object? state) Parameters triggerName string The name identifying the trigger, matching the name used when the operator registered the trigger via RegisterTrigger. state object An optional context object forwarded as-is to each operator's OnTrigger override. Pass null when no additional context is needed. Returns Task A task that completes once the trigger event has been queued on all matching operators. Remarks The engine looks up all operators registered under triggerName and queues a TriggerEvent on each one. If no operator has registered the given trigger name the call is a no-op. The returned task completes once the event has been enqueued on all matching operators, not once their OnTrigger handlers have finished. CallTrigger(string, string, object?) Dispatches a named trigger to a single specific operator within the stream. Task CallTrigger(string operatorName, string triggerName, object? state) Parameters operatorName string The unique name of the target operator within the stream, as set during IStreamVertex.Setup. triggerName string The name identifying the trigger, matching the name used when the operator registered the trigger via RegisterTrigger. state object An optional context object forwarded as-is to the operator's OnTrigger override. Pass null when no additional context is needed. Returns Task A task that completes once the trigger event has been queued on the target operator, or immediately if no matching operator or trigger registration was found. Remarks The engine looks up the operator identified by operatorName and, if it has registered a handler for triggerName, queues a TriggerEvent on it. If the operator does not exist or has not registered the given trigger name the call is a no-op. The returned task completes once the event has been enqueued, not once the operator's OnTrigger handler has finished.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamChe.title: Struct StreamCheckpointNotification | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamChe.summary: Struct StreamCheckpointNotification Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A stack-allocated notification carrying stream checkpoint completion information delivered to ICheckpointListener implementations. public ref struct StreamCheckpointNotification Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks This type is a ref struct to avoid heap allocation on each checkpoint boundary and to allow its fields to be stored as managed references. It must not be stored beyond the duration of the OnCheckpointComplete(StreamCheckpointNotification) call in which it is received. Instances are created internally by StreamNotificationReceiver after the state manager has durably written all operator state for the completed checkpoint. Constructors StreamCheckpointNotification(ref string) Initializes a new StreamCheckpointNotification with a reference to the stream name. public StreamCheckpointNotification(ref string streamName) Parameters streamName string A ref to the stream name string held by StreamNotificationReceiver. Fields StreamName A read-only managed reference to the name of the stream that completed the checkpoint. public readonly ref string StreamName Field Value string

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamFai.title: Struct StreamFailureNotification | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamFai.summary: Struct StreamFailureNotification Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A stack-allocated notification carrying stream failure information delivered to IFailureListener implementations. public ref struct StreamFailureNotification Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks This type is a ref struct to avoid heap allocation on each failure event and to allow its fields to be stored as managed references. It must not be stored beyond the duration of the OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) call in which it is received. Instances are created internally by StreamNotificationReceiver when the stream engine calls StreamContext.OnFailure in response to an unhandled exception. Constructors StreamFailureNotification(ref string, Exception?) Initializes a new StreamFailureNotification with a reference to the stream name and the optional failure exception. public StreamFailureNotification(ref string streamName, Exception? exception) Parameters streamName string A ref to the stream name string held by StreamNotificationReceiver. exception Exception The exception that caused the failure, or null if no specific exception was captured. Fields Exception The exception that caused the failure, or null if no specific exception was captured at the point of failure. public readonly Exception? Exception Field Value Exception StreamName A read-only managed reference to the name of the stream that failed. public readonly ref string StreamName Field Value string

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.title: Class StreamState | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.summary: Class StreamState Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll public class StreamState Inheritance object StreamState Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors StreamState(long, string) public StreamState(long time, string streamHash) Parameters time long streamHash string Fields NullState public static readonly StreamState NullState Field Value StreamState Properties StreamHash public string StreamHash { get; } Property Value string Time The stream time this checkpoint refers to. public long Time { get; } Property Value long

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.summary: Struct StreamStateChangeNotification Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A stack-allocated notification carrying stream state change information delivered to IStreamStateChangeListener implementations. public ref struct StreamStateChangeNotification Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks This type is a ref struct to avoid heap allocation on each state transition and to allow its fields to be stored as managed references. Instances are created internally by StreamNotificationReceiver each time StreamContext transitions to a new StreamStateValue. Constructors StreamStateChangeNotification(ref string, ref StreamStateValue) Initializes a new StreamStateChangeNotification with references to the stream name and the new state value. public StreamStateChangeNotification(ref string streamName, ref StreamStateValue state) Parameters streamName string A ref to the stream name string held by StreamNotificationReceiver. state StreamStateValue A ref to the new StreamStateValue that the stream is entering. Fields State A read-only managed reference to the new StreamStateValue the stream is transitioning into. public readonly ref StreamStateValue State Field Value StreamStateValue StreamName A read-only managed reference to the name of the stream that changed state. public readonly ref string StreamName Field Value string

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.title: Enum StreamStateValue | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.summary: Enum StreamStateValue Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents the high-level lifecycle state of a Flowtide dataflow stream. public enum StreamStateValue Fields Deleted = 5 All persistent state associated with the stream has been permanently removed from storage. This is a terminal state; StopAsync() throws NotSupportedException in this state. Corresponds to Deleted. Deleting = 4 The stream is permanently removing all operator state and persisted data from storage. All dataflow blocks are completed, awaited, disposed, and individually deleted before the stream transitions to Deleted. Corresponds to Deleting. Failure = 3 An unhandled exception has occurred. All running blocks are stopped and disposed, the stream rolls back to the last committed checkpoint, and a restart is automatically scheduled by transitioning back to Starting. This enables automatic self-healing without external intervention. Corresponds to Failing. NotStarted = 0 The stream has not been started, or has returned to an idle state after a graceful stop. This is the initial value assigned when a stream is built via DataflowStreamBuilder, and the state that the stopping sequence transitions back to once all blocks have been completed and disposed. RunAsync() exits when this state is reached. Corresponds to Stopped. Running = 2 The stream is fully initialized and is actively processing data through its operator graph. Triggers are active, checkpoints can be taken, and data flows through all connected vertices. The stream exits this state on a graceful stop request (Stopping), a delete request (Deleting), or an unhandled exception (Failure). Corresponds to Running. Starting = 1 The stream is actively initializing its operator graph, linking dataflow blocks, and restoring operator state from the last committed checkpoint. The stream remains in this state until all egress vertices have completed their initial watermark handshake, at which point it transitions to Running. Any unhandled exception during startup transitions the stream to Failure. Corresponds to Starting. Stopping = 6 A graceful shutdown is in progress. The stream triggers a final checkpoint across all egress vertices, then completes and disposes all blocks before transitioning back to NotStarted. StopAsync() awaits this entire sequence before returning. Corresponds to Stopping. Remarks StreamStateValue tracks the current position within the stream's lifecycle state machine, complementing the finer-grained StreamStatus which reflects the operational condition at a more detailed level. Where StreamStatus surfaces transient conditions such as pausing or degradation, StreamStateValue conveys the coarse lifecycle phase — for example whether the stream is starting up, running normally, recovering from a failure, or shutting down. The current value is exposed via State. Components that need to react to lifecycle transitions can implement IStreamStateChangeListener and register via AddStateChangeListener(IStreamStateChangeListener). Each transition fires all registered listeners synchronously before the associated state machine work begins.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.title: Enum StreamStatus | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.StreamSta.summary: Enum StreamStatus Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents the fine-grained operational status of a Flowtide dataflow stream. public enum StreamStatus Fields Degraded = 3 The stream is operational but experiencing reduced capacity or a transient non-fatal condition, for example database locks from sources. Maps to Degraded. Deleted = 7 All persistent state associated with the stream has been successfully deleted. Set when DeletedStreamState initialization completes. Maps to Unhealthy. Deleting = 6 The stream is permanently removing all operator state from persistent storage. Set during DeletingStreamState initialization. Maps to Unhealthy. Failing = 4 An unhandled exception has occurred. The stream is stopping all running blocks, rolling back to the last committed checkpoint, and preparing to restart automatically. Set during FailureStreamState initialization. Maps to Unhealthy. Paused = 8 Message processing is temporarily suspended via Pause(). Data continues to queue in operator buffers but is not processed until Resume() is called. Maps to Healthy when the underlying StreamStateValue is Running, Unhealthy when it is Failure, and Degraded otherwise. Running = 2 The stream is fully initialized and is actively processing data through its operator graph. Set when the RunningStreamState transition completes successfully. Maps to Healthy. Starting = 1 The stream is initializing its dataflow blocks, linking vertices, and restoring operator state from the last committed checkpoint. Set during the StartStreamState transition. Maps to Degraded. Stopped = 0 The stream has not yet been started or has completed a graceful stop. This is the initial status before StartAsync() is called and the terminal status after a graceful stop sequence completes. Maps to Unhealthy. Stopping = 5 The stream is executing a graceful shutdown sequence, taking a final FlowtideDotNet.Base.StopStreamCheckpoint across all egress vertices before completing. Set during StoppingStreamState initialization. Maps to Unhealthy. Remarks StreamStatus provides more granular operational information than StreamStateValue, which tracks the high-level lifecycle state machine position. Where StreamStateValue reflects the state machine transition (e.g. Starting, Running, Failure), StreamStatus reflects what the stream is actively doing at an operational level, including transient states such as pausing and graceful stopping. The current status is exposed via Status and is also used to derive the FlowtideHealth value reported by Health and the flowtide_status metric gauge. Both StreamStatus and FlowtideHealth are published as observable gauges on the flowtide.{streamName} meter for integration with external monitoring systems.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.html.title: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.html.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine Classes DataflowStream The primary public-facing handle for a Flowtide dataflow stream, providing lifecycle management, monitoring, and operational control over the stream. DataflowStreamBuilder A fluent builder for configuring and constructing a DataflowStream. DefaultStreamScheduler The default IStreamScheduler implementation that dispatches trigger events to stream operators at configurable recurring intervals. StreamState Structs CheckFailureNotification A stack-allocated notification carrying data quality check failure information delivered to ICheckFailureListener implementations. StreamCheckpointNotification A stack-allocated notification carrying stream checkpoint completion information delivered to ICheckpointListener implementations. StreamFailureNotification A stack-allocated notification carrying stream failure information delivered to IFailureListener implementations. StreamStateChangeNotification A stack-allocated notification carrying stream state change information delivered to IStreamStateChangeListener implementations. Interfaces ICheckFailureListener Defines a receiver for data quality check failure notifications raised by the stream. ICheckNotificationReceiver Defines the internal compute-layer sink for data quality check failure events raised directly from compiled SQL CHECK expressions. ICheckpointListener Defines a receiver for checkpoint completion notifications raised by the stream. IFailureListener Defines a receiver for stream failure notifications raised when an unhandled exception causes the stream to transition to a failure state. IStateHandler IStreamScheduler Defines the contract for scheduling and dispatching recurring trigger events to stream operators. IStreamStateChangeListener Defines a receiver for state change notifications raised each time the stream transitions to a new StreamStateValue. IStreamTriggerCaller Defines the contract for dispatching trigger events to one or all operators within a stream. Enums StreamStateValue Represents the high-level lifecycle state of a Flowtide dataflow stream. StreamStatus Represents the fine-grained operational status of a Flowtide dataflow stream.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.FlowtideHealth.h.title: Enum FlowtideHealth | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.FlowtideHealth.h.summary: Enum FlowtideHealth Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents the overall health status of the Flowtide stream. public enum FlowtideHealth Fields Degraded = 1 Indicates that the stream is running but experiencing degraded performance or minor transient errors. Processing may be slower, but the system has not entirely failed. Healthy = 0 Indicates that the stream is fully operational and processing data as expected without issues. Unhealthy = 2 Indicates that the stream has encountered severe errors or critical failures and is no longer able to process data correctly. Remarks This enum is used to report the operational health of the stream, allowing external monitoring systems or health checks to determine if the stream is functioning normally, experiencing problems, or effectively down.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.FlowtidePauseOpt.title: Class FlowtidePauseOptions | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.FlowtidePauseOpt.summary: Class FlowtidePauseOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents the options to dynamically pause and resume a Flowtide dataflow stream. public class FlowtidePauseOptions Inheritance object FlowtidePauseOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This class is typically used in conjunction with IOptionsMonitor<FlowtidePauseOptions>. By subscribing to changes via the options monitor, the engine can halt or resume event propagation at runtime without needing to tear down or restart the stream. Properties IsPaused Gets or sets a value indicating whether the stream should be paused. public bool IsPaused { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks When set to true, the stream will temporarily stop processing new events. Changing this value to false will cause the stream to resume normal processing operations.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.ICheckpointEvent.title: Interface ICheckpointEvent | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.ICheckpointEvent.summary: Interface ICheckpointEvent Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents a checkpoint event within the dataflow stream. public interface ICheckpointEvent : ILockingEvent, IStreamEvent Remarks Checkpoint events are propagated through the stream to coordinate state persistence and synchronization across different vertices and operators. This ensures fault tolerance and data consistency. Properties CheckpointTime Gets the time of the checkpoint being finalized. long CheckpointTime { get; } Property Value long NewTime Gets the time of the newly created checkpoint span. long NewTime { get; } Property Value long

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.ILockingEvent.ht.title: Interface ILockingEvent | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.ILockingEvent.ht.summary: Interface ILockingEvent Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents an event within the dataflow stream that requires operators and vertices to halt standard processing and synchronize their state. public interface ILockingEvent : IStreamEvent Remarks Locking events are used for critical operations such as taking checkpoints. When a sequence of locking events is propagated, it ensures that data is consistently processed and stored up to that point across all branches of the stream before proceeding.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.IRentable.html.title: Interface IRentable | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.IRentable.html.summary: Interface IRentable Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Represents an object or resource whose lifetime is managed through reference counting. Used heavily to track memory allocations across the stream and return resources to pools. public interface IRentable Methods Rent(int) Increases the reference count of the rentable resource by the specified amount. Call this when the resource is passed to a new scope or consumer that will rely on it. void Rent(int count) Parameters count int The number of additional references to acquire. Return() Decreases the reference count of the rentable resource by one. When the count reaches zero, the underlying resource may be disposed, freed, or returned to a pool. void Return()

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils.EmptyAsync.title: Class EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils.EmptyAsync.summary: Class EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A zero-allocation async enumerable that always yields zero elements of type T. public class EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> : IAsyncEnumerable<T> Type Parameters T The element type declared by this enumerable, though no elements are ever produced. Inheritance object EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> Implements IAsyncEnumerable<T> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Both the enumerable and its inner enumerator are singletons. Instance is a shared static instance of the enumerable, and GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken) always returns the same static Enumerator instance. This means the type produces no heap allocations at all, whether accessed via Instance or by calling GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken). It is used throughout the dataflow vertex pipeline wherever a method returning IAsyncEnumerable<T> must produce no output, for example when a checkpoint or watermark handler chooses to suppress output without starting an iterator state machine. For the single-element counterpart see SingleAsyncEnumerable<T>. Fields Instance Gets the shared singleton instance of EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> for the given type argument. public static readonly EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> Instance Field Value EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T> Remarks Prefer using this field over constructing a new instance to avoid any heap allocation. Methods GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken) Returns the enumerator for this empty sequence. public IAsyncEnumerator<T> GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken The token to monitor for cancellation requests. Not observed by this implementation. Returns IAsyncEnumerator<T> The shared static IAsyncEnumerator<T> that immediately signals end-of-sequence. Remarks Always returns the shared static Enumerator instance, producing no heap allocation. The returned enumerator's MoveNextAsync immediately returns false, and its Current property always yields default(T).

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils.SingleAsyn.title: Class SingleAsyncEnumerable<T> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils.SingleAsyn.summary: Class SingleAsyncEnumerable<T> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll A single-allocation async enumerable that yields exactly one element of type T. public class SingleAsyncEnumerable<T> : IAsyncEnumerable<T>, IAsyncEnumerator<T>, IAsyncDisposable Type Parameters T The type of the single element produced by this enumerable. Inheritance object SingleAsyncEnumerable<T> Implements IAsyncEnumerable<T> IAsyncEnumerator<T> IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This class implements both IAsyncEnumerable<T> and IAsyncEnumerator<T> on the same instance to avoid a secondary heap allocation for the enumerator. As a result, GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken) returns this and the class is not safe for concurrent enumeration and must not be enumerated more than once. It is used throughout the dataflow vertex pipeline wherever a method returning IAsyncEnumerable<T> must produce exactly one output item, for example when a checkpoint handler or a single-result OnRecieve override needs to emit a single IStreamEvent without the overhead of a full iterator state machine. For the zero-element counterpart see EmptyAsyncEnumerable<T>. Constructors SingleAsyncEnumerable(T) Initializes a new instance of SingleAsyncEnumerable<T> that will yield value exactly once. public SingleAsyncEnumerable(T value) Parameters value T The single element to be produced during enumeration. Properties Current Gets the current element in the enumeration. public T Current { get; } Property Value T Remarks This property always returns the value supplied at construction time, regardless of the current iteration state. It should only be accessed after a successful call to MoveNextAsync(). Methods DisposeAsync() Performs any cleanup required when the enumerator is no longer needed. This implementation is a no-op and completes synchronously. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A completed ValueTask. GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken) Returns the enumerator for this sequence. public IAsyncEnumerator<T> GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken The token to monitor for cancellation requests. Not observed by this implementation. Returns IAsyncEnumerator<T> This instance as IAsyncEnumerator<T>. Remarks Because this class implements IAsyncEnumerator<T> directly, this method returns this instance. Calling this method more than once or enumerating concurrently produces undefined behaviour. MoveNextAsync() Advances the enumerator to the single element of the sequence. public ValueTask<bool> MoveNextAsync() Returns ValueTask<bool> A ValueTask<TResult> that resolves to true the first time it is called, and false on all subsequent calls. Remarks Returns true on the first call, making Current available. Returns false on every subsequent call, signalling that enumeration is complete.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.EgressV.title: Class EgressVertex<T> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.EgressV.summary: Class EgressVertex<T> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Abstract base class for stream egress vertices (sinks) that consume stream events from the dataflow pipeline. public abstract class EgressVertex<T> : ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent>, IDataflowBlock, IStreamEgressVertex, IStreamVertex, IAsyncDisposable Type Parameters T The type of the underlying data payload consumed by this vertex. Inheritance object EgressVertex<T> Implements ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent> IDataflowBlock IStreamEgressVertex IStreamVertex IAsyncDisposable Derived ColumnGroupedWriteOperator GroupedWriteBaseOperator WriteBaseOperator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This vertex acts as an ITargetBlock<TInput> in the TPL Dataflow pipeline, terminating a stream branch by receiving and handling all incoming messages without producing output. It handles common stream lifecycle events: state initialization or restoration, processing messages, recording watermark latency, managing checkpoints, and supporting trigger execution. Depending on whether parallel execution is enabled via ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions, the internal implementation dispatches to either a parallel or a non-parallel egress block. Derived classes must implement custom data sink logic via OnRecieve(T, long) and persist any required state during checkpoints via OnCheckpoint(long). When a checkpoint completes, the vertex notifies the stream engine automatically through an internal callback registered by the stream infrastructure. When parallel execution is enabled, a ParallelEgressVertex is used internally; otherwise a NonParallelEgressVertex is used. CurrentCheckpointId Gets the logical checkpoint time identifier of the most recently processed checkpoint. public long CurrentCheckpointId { get; } Property Value long DisplayName Gets the display name for the specific vertex type, used mainly in logging and metrics visualization. public abstract string DisplayName { get; } Property Value string Logger Gets the logger instance associated with this vertex. public ILogger Logger { get; } Property Value ILogger Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessed before Initialize(string, long, long, IVertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation?) has been called. Metrics Gets the meter instance used to create metrics for this vertex. protected IMeter Metrics { get; } Property Value IMeter Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessed before Initialize(string, long, long, IVertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation?) has been called. Name Gets the unique configured operator name of the vertex. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessed before Setup(string, string) or Initialize(string, long, long, IVertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation?) has been called. StreamName Gets the name of the stream this vertex belongs to. protected string StreamName { get; } Property Value string Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessed before Setup(string, string) or Initialize(string, long, long, IVertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation?) has been called. StreamVersion Gets the version information of the currently running stream. public StreamVersionInformation?
