Returns immediately if the vertex is not currently paused. protected ValueTask CheckForPause() Returns ValueTask A ValueTask that completes when the vertex is resumed, or immediately if not paused. Returns Task Compact() Requests the operator to compact its persistent storage. public abstract Task Compact() Returns Task A task representing the compaction process. Must be called before messages can be offered or before Completion is accessed. public void CreateBlock() DeleteAsync() Initiates the deletion process, allowing derived classes to permanently clean up any persistent state associated with this vertex. public abstract Task DeleteAsync() Returns Task A task representing the delete operation. DisposeAsync() Releases internal resources held by this vertex asynchronously. public virtual ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask FailAndRollback(Exception?, long?) Requests the stream engine to fail and initiate a rollback to a previous safe state. protected Task FailAndRollback(Exception? exception = null, long? restoreVersion = null) Parameters exception Exception An optional exception describing the reason for the failure. restoreVersion long? An optional specific version to roll back to; if null, the latest checkpoint is used. Returns Task A task representing the fail-and-rollback operation. Fault(Exception) Puts the underlying dataflow block immediately into a faulted state due to a severe exception. public void Fault(Exception exception) Parameters exception Exception The exception that caused the fault. GetLinks() Returns all downstream ITargetBlock<TInput> linked from this vertex. Egress vertices have no downstream links and always return an empty enumerable. public IEnumerable<ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent>> GetLinks() Returns IEnumerable<ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent>> An empty enumerable, as egress vertices have no output links. Initialize(string, long, long, IVertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation?) Asynchronously initializes the vertex, wiring up metrics, memory, logging, and persistent state retrieval. public Task Initialize(string name, long restoreTime, long newTime, IVertexHandler vertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation? streamVersionInformation) Parameters name string The name assigned to this vertex. restoreTime long The logical time representing the last known good state to restore from. newTime long The new logical stream execution time after initialization. vertexHandler IVertexHandler The handler providing state clients, memory managers, logger factories, and metrics. streamVersionInformation StreamVersionInformation Optional version information used to handle stream upgrades or downgrades. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous initialization and state restore operation. Returns Task A task representing the trigger handling operation. OnWatermark(Watermark) Called when a Watermark arrives at this egress vertex. Allows derived classes to react to watermark advancement, for example to flush buffered output per time boundary. protected virtual Task OnWatermark(Watermark watermark) Parameters watermark Watermark The watermark event received from upstream. Returns Task A task representing the watermark handling operation. api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.FixedPo.title: Class FixedPointVertex<T> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.FixedPo.summary: Class FixedPointVertex<T> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Abstract base class for stream vertices that implement fixed-point iteration over a cyclic dataflow graph. public abstract class FixedPointVertex<T> : IStreamVertex, IAsyncDisposable Type Parameters T The type of the underlying data payload processed by this vertex. Inheritance object FixedPointVertex<T> Implements IStreamVertex IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks A fixed-point vertex coordinates two distinct input sources — an ingress input carrying new external data and a feedback input carrying data recycled from the loop — and routes processed results to two output sources: an egress output forwarding results downstream and a loop output feeding data back into the cycle. Derived classes express the per-message processing logic via OnIngressRecieve(T, long) and OnFeedbackRecieve(T, long), mapping each output event to index 0 (egress) or 1 (loop). Checkpointing differs fundamentally from MultipleInputVertex<T>: when a checkpoint arrives on the ingress input, the vertex sends a FlowtideDotNet.Base.LockingEventPrepare into the loop and waits for the loop to confirm that no new messages are in flight before propagating the checkpoint to the egress output. The method NoReadSourceInLoop() controls this behaviour — returning true indicates no persistent read source exists in the loop, allowing the checkpoint to complete without waiting for an upstream acknowledgement. Constructors FixedPointVertex(ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions) Initializes a new instance of FixedPointVertex<T> with the specified execution options. public FixedPointVertex(ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions executionDataflowBlockOptions) Parameters executionDataflowBlockOptions ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions Configuration options governing block capacities and parallel properties. The bounded capacity is scaled up internally to accommodate both ingress and feedback buffering. Properties Completion Gets a Task that represents the asynchronous completion of the vertex's internal processing block. public Task Completion { get; } Property Value Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessed before CreateBlock() has been called. DisplayName Gets the display name for the specific vertex type, used mainly in logging and metrics visualization. public abstract string DisplayName { get; } Property Value string EgressSource Gets the ISourceBlock<TOutput> that emits processed results downstream out of the loop. public ISourceBlock<IStreamEvent> EgressSource { get; } Property Value ISourceBlock<IStreamEvent> FeedbackTarget Gets the ITargetBlock<TInput> that accepts recycled data fed back from the loop output. public ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent> FeedbackTarget { get; } Property Value ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent> IngressTarget Gets the ITargetBlock<TInput> that accepts new external data entering the fixed-point loop. public ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent> IngressTarget { get; } Property Value ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent> Logger Gets the logger instance associated with this vertex. public ILogger Logger { get; } Property Value ILogger LoopSource Gets the ISourceBlock<TOutput> that emits data back into the loop for the next iteration. public ISourceBlock<IStreamEvent> LoopSource { get; } Property Value ISourceBlock<IStreamEvent> MemoryAllocator Gets the memory allocator for managing native memory allocations within this vertex. protected IMemoryAllocator MemoryAllocator { get; } Property Value IMemoryAllocator Metrics Gets the meter instance used to create metrics for this vertex. protected IMeter Metrics { get; } Property Value IMeter Name Gets the unique configured operator name of the vertex. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when accessed before Setup(string, string) or Initialize(string, long, long, IVertexHandler, StreamVersionInformation?) has been called. Methods BeforeSaveCheckpoint() Called before the checkpoint state is persisted. Allows the vertex to flush any pending state. public virtual Task BeforeSaveCheckpoint() Returns Task A task representing the pre-checkpoint operation. Compact() Requests the operator to compact its persistent storage. public virtual Task Compact() Returns Task A task representing the compaction process. This method should be used sparingly since the vertex might have handled data that is after the checkpoint, so the vertex need to take that into consideration. One use case is if a source has an offset (such as Kafka), and only a subset of events are in the result set. If no new event has been sent into the stream after the checkpoint event, this method can be used to update the offset to the latest one to skip loading in unnecessary data in the case of a crash. api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.Multipl.summary: Class MultipleInputTargetHolder Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Holder that represents one input slot of a MultipleInputVertex<T>, implementing ITargetBlock<TInput> so that upstream sources can be linked directly to it. public class MultipleInputTargetHolder : ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent>, IDataflowBlock Inheritance object MultipleInputTargetHolder Implements ITargetBlock<IStreamEvent> IDataflowBlock Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Each instance wraps a lazily initialized FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.MultipleInputTarget<T> and is identified by a zero-based targetId that determines which input index is being fed when messages arrive at the parent vertex. The internal FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.MultipleInputTarget<T> is created during the vertex setup phase via Initialize, and the holder must be fully set up (via Setup) before the OperatorName and Completion members are accessible. Upstream dataflow blocks are connected to this holder using standard TPL Dataflow link mechanics, which the holder forwards to the underlying target block. api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.Multipl.title: Class MultipleInputVertex<T> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.Multipl.summary: Class MultipleInputVertex<T> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll Vertex capable of receiving and processing input from multiple distinct upstream sources concurrently. public abstract class MultipleInputVertex<T> : ISourceBlock<IStreamEvent>, IDataflowBlock, IStreamVertex, IAsyncDisposable Type Parameters T The underlying data type of the stream messages being processed. Inheritance object MultipleInputVertex<T> Implements ISourceBlock<IStreamEvent> IDataflowBlock IStreamVertex IAsyncDisposable Derived PartitionedOutputVertex<T> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This vertex acts as an ISourceBlock<TOutput> emitting a unified output after accepting and coordinating multiple inputs. It heavily manages stream coordination by synchronizing Watermarks, ILockingEvents (checkpoints), and FlowtideDotNet.Base.InitialDataDoneEvents across all configured input targets. This ensures the operator advances its logical state only when all discrete input branches have reached an agreed-upon synchronization point. api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.html.title: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.html.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices Classes EgressVertex<T> Abstract base class for stream egress vertices (sinks) that consume stream events from the dataflow pipeline. FixedPointVertex<T> Abstract base class for stream vertices that implement fixed-point iteration over a cyclic dataflow graph. IngressOutput<T> Provides an output for an IngressVertex<TData> to push data and watermarks into the stream. IngressVertex<TData> Base class for stream ingress vertices (sources) that produce stream events into the dataflow pipeline. MultipleInputTargetHolder Holder that represents one input slot of a MultipleInputVertex<T>, implementing ITargetBlock<TInput> so that upstream sources can be linked directly to it. MultipleInputVertex<T> Vertex capable of receiving and processing input from multiple distinct upstream sources concurrently. PartitionVertex<T> Vertex responsible for partitioning an input stream into multiple target streams. PartitionedOutputVertex<T> Represents a specialized vertex that gathers data from multiple upstream partitions and merges them back into a single continuous stream. UnaryVertex<T> Base class for stream vertices that have a single input stream and one or more output streams. Interfaces IStreamEgressVertex Represents an egress node (sink operator) within the Flowtide dataflow stream. IStreamIngressVertex Represents an ingress node (source operator) within the Flowtide dataflow stream. IStreamVertex Represents a foundational node (operator or vertex) within the Flowtide dataflow stream. Defines lifecycle methods (Setup, Initialize, CreateBlock, Link), operational hooks (Pause, Resume, Compact), and core metadata properties.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Base.html.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Base Namespaces FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine FlowtideDotNet.Base.Metrics FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices Classes AbstractWatermarkValue Base abstract class for watermark values used in the dataflow stream to track data progression. AbstractWatermarkValue<T> Typed base class for specific watermark values in the dataflow stream. Checkpoint Represents a checkpoint event within the dataflow stream. FlowtidePauseOptions Represents the options to dynamically pause and resume a Flowtide dataflow stream. LongWatermarkValue Watermark value backed by a 64-bit integer (long). StreamMessage Base abstract class for data-carrying messages flowing through the stream. StreamMessage<T> Strongly-typed data message flowing through the stream. TriggerEvent Represents an event that should invoke a registered trigger within the stream. Watermark Event that contains watermarks from the sources. Each source has unique watermarks to allow the egress to keep track of which offset from each source it is finished with. WatermarkSerializeFactory Provides a global registry mapping integer type IDs to IWatermarkSerializer implementations. This allows custom watermark types to be correctly serialized and deserialized across the stream engine. Interfaces ICheckpointEvent Represents a checkpoint event within the dataflow stream. ILockingEvent Represents an event within the dataflow stream that requires operators and vertices to halt standard processing and synchronize their state. IRentable Represents an object or resource whose lifetime is managed through reference counting. Used heavily to track memory allocations across the stream and return resources to pools. IStreamEvent The base interface for all events that can flow through a stream. IVertexHandler Provides context and operational hooks for a stream vertex (operator) allowing it to interact with the underlying stream engine, manage state, log information, and report metrics. IWatermarkSerializer Provides a mechanism to serialize and deserialize watermark values. This is used when transferring watermarks across network boundaries (such as between partitions) or when persisting them. Enums FlowtideHealth Represents the overall health status of the Flowtide stream.

Only substreams recorded as started are reached: a stopped stream has no recorded substreams, to delete its state start it again first and then delete. Task DeleteStreamAsync() Returns Task GetStatusAsync() Returns the status of the stream and each substream it started, including start failures that happened in the background after StartStreamAsync(StartStreamRequest) returned. Task<StreamStatusResponse> GetStatusAsync() Returns Task<StreamStatusResponse> StartStreamAsync(StartStreamRequest) Starts all substreams. Starting an already started stream with the same SQL and substream count is a no-op; a different plan throws (stop it first). Returns once the grains accept the start; the streams start in the background, poll GetStatusAsync() for health. Task StartStreamAsync(StartStreamRequest request) Parameters request StartStreamRequest Returns Task StopStreamAsync() Stops all substreams together, so the coordinated stop drains the data exchanged between them and everything sent before the stop is part of the final checkpoints. The grain persists which substreams it started, so the caller supplies nothing. Task StopStreamAsync() Returns Task

api/FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Messa.title: Class SubstreamEventsPayload | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Messa.summary: Class SubstreamEventsPayload Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Messages Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.dll Fetched substream events, serialized only when they actually cross a silo boundary. The serving grain hands the live events over by reference; between grains on the same silo the payload instance passes through Orleans uncopied (identity copier) and the receiver consumes the live events with zero copies, taking over the receiver claim exactly like a local fetch. Only when Orleans serializes the message does the codec write the events in the substream wire format, releasing the claims on the sending side; the receiving silo captures the raw payload without materializing it, and the communication handler deserializes lazily with the consuming operator's allocator. Ownership is one-shot: exactly one consumer takes the live events (the same-silo receiver or the serializing codec). A payload is bound to one response. public sealed class SubstreamEventsPayload Inheritance object SubstreamEventsPayload Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties IsSerialized True when the payload crossed a silo boundary and holds the wire format instead of live events. public bool IsSerialized { get; } Property Value bool Methods FromLive(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>) Wraps live events for a fetch response. The payload takes over the receiver claims; the caller must not touch the events afterwards. public static SubstreamEventsPayload FromLive(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> events) Parameters events IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> Returns SubstreamEventsPayload TryTakeLive(out IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>) Takes the live events and their receiver claims. One-shot: the same-silo receiver and the serializing codec race at most conceptually, never actually, but the exchange keeps a bug from turning into a double release of the claims. public bool TryTakeLive(out IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> events) Parameters events IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> Returns bool

SubstreamEventsPayload Fetched substream events, serialized only when they actually cross a silo boundary. The serving grain hands the live events over by reference; between grains on the same silo the payload instance passes through Orleans uncopied (identity copier) and the receiver consumes the live events with zero copies, taking over the receiver claim exactly like a local fetch. Only when Orleans serializes the message does the codec write the events in the substream wire format, releasing the claims on the sending side; the receiving silo captures the raw payload without materializing it, and the communication handler deserializes lazily with the consuming operator's allocator. Ownership is one-shot: exactly one consumer takes the live events (the same-silo receiver or the serializing codec). A payload is bound to one response. SubstreamStatus Status of one substream, reported by its substream grain.

Files are usually smaller than this value because of compression. public long MaxFileSizeBytes { get; set; } Property Value long Remarks This setting is used to determine when to roll over to a new file during writes. If the current file being written exceeds this size, a new file will be created for subsequent data. Setting this value too low may result in many small files, which can degrade read performance, while setting it too high may lead to fewer but larger files, which can also impact performance and manageability. The default value is set to 100 MB. api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbConsistency | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbConsistency Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents the consistency requirements for a SpiceDB API request, controlling how data freshness is balanced against latency. public interface ISpiceDbConsistency Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 Consistency message. Each request must specify exactly one consistency requirement. Use the static factory methods (CreateFullyConsistent(), CreateMinimizeLatency(), CreateAtExactSnapshot(ISpiceDbZedToken), CreateAtLeastAsFresh(ISpiceDbZedToken)) to create instances. Properties AtExactSnapshot Gets or sets the ISpiceDbZedToken that specifies the exact snapshot at which all data used in the API call must be evaluated. ISpiceDbZedToken AtExactSnapshot { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbZedToken Remarks If the specified snapshot is no longer available, an error will be returned to the caller. AtLeastAsFresh Gets or sets the ISpiceDbZedToken that specifies the minimum freshness of data used in the API call. ISpiceDbZedToken AtLeastAsFresh { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbZedToken Remarks All data used in the API call must be at least as fresh as that found in the provided ISpiceDbZedToken. More recent data may be used if it is available or faster. FullyConsistent Gets or sets a value indicating that all data used in the API call must be at the most recent snapshot found. bool FullyConsistent { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks Using this option can be quite slow. Unless there is a specific need to enforce full consistency, it is recommended to use AtLeastAsFresh with a stored ISpiceDbZedToken instead. HasFullyConsistent Gets a value indicating whether FullyConsistent has been set on this instance. bool HasFullyConsistent { get; } Property Value bool HasMinimizeLatency Gets a value indicating whether MinimizeLatency has been set on this instance. bool HasMinimizeLatency { get; } Property Value bool MinimizeLatency Gets or sets a value indicating that the latency for the call should be minimized by having the system select the fastest snapshot available. bool MinimizeLatency { get; set; } Property Value bool Methods CreateAtExactSnapshot(ISpiceDbZedToken) Creates an ISpiceDbConsistency instance that requires all data used in the API call to be evaluated at the exact snapshot identified by zedToken. public static ISpiceDbConsistency CreateAtExactSnapshot(ISpiceDbZedToken zedToken) Parameters zedToken ISpiceDbZedToken The ISpiceDbZedToken identifying the exact snapshot to use. If the snapshot is no longer available, the API call will return an error. Returns ISpiceDbConsistency An ISpiceDbConsistency with AtExactSnapshot set to zedToken. CreateAtLeastAsFresh(ISpiceDbZedToken) Creates an ISpiceDbConsistency instance that requires all data used in the API call to be at least as fresh as the snapshot identified by zedToken. public static ISpiceDbConsistency CreateAtLeastAsFresh(ISpiceDbZedToken zedToken) Parameters zedToken ISpiceDbZedToken The ISpiceDbZedToken identifying the minimum required freshness. More recent data may be used if it is available or faster. Returns ISpiceDbConsistency An ISpiceDbConsistency with AtLeastAsFresh set to zedToken. CreateFullyConsistent() Creates an ISpiceDbConsistency instance that requires all data used in the API call to be at the most recent snapshot found. public static ISpiceDbConsistency CreateFullyConsistent() Returns ISpiceDbConsistency An ISpiceDbConsistency with FullyConsistent set to true. CreateMinimizeLatency() Creates an ISpiceDbConsistency instance that minimizes latency by having the system select the fastest available snapshot. public static ISpiceDbConsistency CreateMinimizeLatency() Returns ISpiceDbConsistency An ISpiceDbConsistency with MinimizeLatency set to true.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a caveat attached to a caveated relationship in SpiceDB, combining a caveat expression name with its evaluation context. public interface ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 ContextualizedCaveat message. A caveated relationship is considered active only when the referenced caveat expression evaluates to true given the supplied context. Instances are assigned via OptionalCaveat. Use the static Create(string?, Struct?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties CaveatName Gets or sets the name of the caveat expression to use, as defined in the SpiceDB schema. string CaveatName { get; set; } Property Value string Context Gets or sets the key-value pairs that are injected into the caveat expression at evaluation time. Struct Context { get; set; } Property Value Struct Remarks The keys must match the arguments defined for the caveat in the SpiceDB schema. These values are fixed at write time and passed to the caveat expression when the relationship is evaluated. Methods Create(string?, Struct?) Creates a new ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat instance with the specified caveat name and evaluation context. public static ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat Create(string? caveatName = null, Struct? context = null) Parameters caveatName string The name of the caveat expression to use, as defined in the SpiceDB schema. If null, the caveat name will not be set. context Struct The key-value pairs to inject into the caveat expression at evaluation time. The keys must match the arguments defined for the caveat in the schema. If null, no context will be set. Returns ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat A new ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat with the specified caveat name and context.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbObjectReference | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbObjectReference Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a reference to a specific object in the SpiceDB permission system, identified by its type and unique identifier. public interface ISpiceDbObjectReference Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 ObjectReference message. Object references identify the resource side of a relationship via Resource, and form the object component of a subject reference via Object. Use the static Create(string?, string?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties ObjectId Gets or sets the unique identifier of the object within its type. string ObjectId { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks Supports the wildcard value "*" to match all objects of the given type when used in relationship filters. ObjectType Gets or sets the type of the object, as defined in the SpiceDB schema (for example, "document" or "user"). string ObjectType { get; set; } Property Value string Methods Create(string?, string?) Creates a new ISpiceDbObjectReference instance with the specified object type and identifier. public static ISpiceDbObjectReference Create(string? objectType = null, string? objectId = null) Parameters objectType string The type of the object as defined in the SpiceDB schema (for example, "document"). If null, the object type will not be set. objectId string The unique identifier of the object within its type. If null, the object identifier will not be set. Returns ISpiceDbObjectReference A new ISpiceDbObjectReference with the specified object type and identifier.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbPrecondition | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbPrecondition Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a precondition that must be satisfied before a SpiceDB write operation is committed. public interface ISpiceDbPrecondition Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 Precondition message. Preconditions assert the existence or absence of relationships that match a given filter; if any precondition is not satisfied, the parent write is not committed. Preconditions are supplied via OptionalPreconditions. Use the static Create(SpiceDbPreconditionOperation?, ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties Filter Gets or sets the relationship filter used to evaluate this precondition. ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter Filter { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter Operation Gets or sets the operation that determines how the filter match result affects whether the parent request is allowed to proceed. SpiceDbPreconditionOperation Operation { get; set; } Property Value SpiceDbPreconditionOperation Methods Create(SpiceDbPreconditionOperation?, ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter?) Creates a new ISpiceDbPrecondition instance with the specified operation and relationship filter. public static ISpiceDbPrecondition Create(SpiceDbPreconditionOperation? operation = null, ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter? filter = null) Parameters operation SpiceDbPreconditionOperation? The SpiceDbPreconditionOperation that determines how the filter result affects the parent request. If null, the operation will not be set. filter ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter The ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter used to evaluate this precondition. If null, the filter will not be set. Returns ISpiceDbPrecondition A new ISpiceDbPrecondition with the specified operation and filter.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbReadRelationshipsRequest | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbReadRelationshipsRequest Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a request to read relationships from SpiceDB that match one or more filters. public interface ISpiceDbReadRelationshipsRequest Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 ReadRelationshipsRequest message. It is used to query SpiceDB for existing relationships via the ReadRelationships RPC, and is also accepted by DeleteExistingDataFilter to identify relationships that should be removed after the initial data load has completed. Use the static Create(ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter, ISpiceDbConsistency?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties Consistency Gets or sets the consistency requirement for the read operation. ISpiceDbConsistency Consistency { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbConsistency Remarks When null, the SpiceDB server applies its default consistency behaviour. Use ISpiceDbConsistency factory methods to specify an explicit requirement. RelationshipFilter Gets or sets the filter applied to the relationships returned by the read operation. ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter RelationshipFilter { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter Methods Create(ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter, ISpiceDbConsistency?) Relationships form the data for the graph over which all permissions questions are answered. They are carried as the Relationship field when writing to SpiceDB via ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest. Use the static Create(ISpiceDbObjectReference?, string?, ISpiceDbSubjectReference?, ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat?) factory method to construct a new instance. When set, the caveat expression named by CaveatName is evaluated with the provided context at permission-check time. ResourceType is required; all other properties are optional and impose no additional constraint when left unset. A filter is supplied as RelationshipFilter to scope a read operation, and as Filter to define the relationship set evaluated by a precondition. Use the static Create(string?, string?, string?, ISpiceDbSubjectFilter?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties OptionalRelation Gets or sets an optional relation name to match (for example, "reader"). string? OptionalRelation { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks When not set, relationships for any relation on the resource type are returned. OptionalResourceId Gets or sets an optional resource identifier to match. string OptionalResourceId { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks When not set, relationships for any identifier of the given resource type are returned. OptionalSubjectFilter Gets or sets an optional subject filter to further narrow results by subject type, identifier, or sub-relation. ISpiceDbSubjectFilter OptionalSubjectFilter { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbSubjectFilter Remarks When not set, no additional constraint is applied to the subject side of the matched relationships. ResourceType Gets or sets the type of the resource to filter on, as defined in the SpiceDB schema (for example, "document"). This field is required. string ResourceType { get; set; } Property Value string Methods Create(string?, string?, string?, ISpiceDbSubjectFilter?) If null, the resource type will not be set. optionalResourceId string The specific resource identifier to match. If null, the resource identifier filter will not be applied. optionalRelation string The relation name to match (for example, "reader"). If null, the relation filter will not be applied. optionalSubjectFilter ISpiceDbSubjectFilter An ISpiceDbSubjectFilter to further narrow results by subject type, identifier, or sub-relation. Relationship updates are submitted in batches via Updates. Use the static Create(SpiceDbRelationshipUpdateOperation?, ISpiceDbRelationship?) factory method to construct a new instance. Touch upserts the relationship and succeeds even if it already exists. Delete removes the relationship and no-ops if it does not exist. api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbSubjectFilter | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbSubjectFilter Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a filter on the subject side of a relationship in SpiceDB. public interface ISpiceDbSubjectFilter Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 SubjectFilter message. SubjectType is required; all other properties are optional and impose no additional constraint when left unset. A subject filter is supplied as OptionalSubjectFilter to narrow a relationship query by the type, identifier, or sub-relation of its subject side. Use the static Create(string?, string?, ISpiceDbRelationFilter?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties OptionalRelation Gets or sets an optional sub-relation filter to further narrow results by the relation held by the subject. ISpiceDbRelationFilter OptionalRelation { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbRelationFilter Remarks When not set, no additional constraint is applied to the sub-relation of the matched subjects. OptionalSubjectId Gets or sets an optional subject identifier to match. string? OptionalSubjectId { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks When not set, subjects of any identifier matching the given type are returned. Supports the wildcard value "*" to match all subjects of the given type. SubjectType Gets or sets the type of the subject to filter on, as defined in the SpiceDB schema (for example, "user"). This field is required. string SubjectType { get; set; } Property Value string Methods Create(string?, string?, ISpiceDbRelationFilter?) Creates a new ISpiceDbSubjectFilter instance with the specified subject type and optional narrowing criteria. public static ISpiceDbSubjectFilter Create(string? subjectType = null, string? optionalSubjectId = null, ISpiceDbRelationFilter? optionalRelation = null) Parameters subjectType string The type of the subject to filter on, as defined in the SpiceDB schema (for example, "user"). If null, the subject type will not be set. optionalSubjectId string The specific subject identifier to match. If null, the subject identifier filter will not be applied. optionalRelation ISpiceDbRelationFilter An ISpiceDbRelationFilter to further narrow results by the sub-relation held by the subject. If null, no sub-relation filter will be applied. Returns ISpiceDbSubjectFilter A new ISpiceDbSubjectFilter with the specified subject type and narrowing criteria.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbSubjectReference | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbSubjectReference Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a reference to the subject portion of a SpiceDB relationship, identifying a subject by its object reference and an optional sub-relation. public interface ISpiceDbSubjectReference Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 SubjectReference message. A subject reference is used as Subject to identify who holds a relation on a resource. The optional relation component enables referencing a sub-relation on the subject — for example, group:engineering#members to refer to the members of a group rather than the group itself. Use the static Create(ISpiceDbObjectReference?, string?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties Object Gets or sets the object reference that identifies the subject. ISpiceDbObjectReference Object { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbObjectReference OptionalRelation Gets or sets an optional sub-relation on the subject that further qualifies who the subject is (for example, "members" to refer to the members of a group). string OptionalRelation { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks When not set, the subject is the object identified by Object directly. When set, the subject is the set of objects reachable via the named relation on the referenced object. Methods Create(ISpiceDbObjectReference?, string?) Creates a new ISpiceDbSubjectReference instance with the specified object reference and optional sub-relation. public static ISpiceDbSubjectReference Create(ISpiceDbObjectReference? obj = null, string? optionalRelation = null) Parameters obj ISpiceDbObjectReference The ISpiceDbObjectReference identifying the subject. If null, the object reference will not be set. optionalRelation string The name of the sub-relation on the subject (for example, "members"). If null, no sub-relation will be set and the subject refers to the object identified by obj directly. Returns ISpiceDbSubjectReference A new ISpiceDbSubjectReference with the specified object reference and optional sub-relation.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a request to atomically apply a set of relationship mutations to SpiceDB, with optional preconditions. public interface ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 WriteRelationshipsRequest message. The mutations in Updates are submitted to SpiceDB via the WriteRelationships RPC. When OptionalPreconditions is non-empty, all specified preconditions must be satisfied before the write is committed; if any precondition fails the entire write is rejected. The request is also passed to BeforeWriteRequestFunc before each batch submission, allowing callers to inspect or modify the updates prior to sending. Use the static Create(IEnumerable<ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate>?, IEnumerable<ISpiceDbPrecondition>?) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties OptionalPreconditions Gets the mutable list of preconditions that must all be satisfied before the mutations in Updates are committed. IList<ISpiceDbPrecondition> OptionalPreconditions { get; } Property Value IList<ISpiceDbPrecondition> Remarks When empty, no preconditions are evaluated and the write proceeds unconditionally. If any precondition is not satisfied, the entire write is rejected without applying any of the mutations. Updates Gets the mutable list of relationship mutations to apply atomically. IList<ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate> Updates { get; } Property Value IList<ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate> Remarks Add ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate instances to specify which relationships to create, upsert, or delete as part of this request. Methods Create(IEnumerable<ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate>?, IEnumerable<ISpiceDbPrecondition>?) Creates a new ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest instance pre-populated with the specified relationship updates and optional preconditions. public static ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest Create(IEnumerable<ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate>? updates = null, IEnumerable<ISpiceDbPrecondition>? optionalPreconditions = null) Parameters updates IEnumerable<ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate> The ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate mutations to include in the request. If null, no updates will be added. optionalPreconditions IEnumerable<ISpiceDbPrecondition> The ISpiceDbPrecondition conditions that must all be satisfied before the write is committed. If null, no preconditions will be added and the write proceeds unconditionally. Returns ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest A new ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest containing the specified updates and preconditions.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.title: Interface ISpiceDbZedToken | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISp.summary: Interface ISpiceDbZedToken Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Represents a ZedToken that encodes causality metadata for SpiceDB read and write operations. public interface ISpiceDbZedToken Remarks This interface wraps the Authzed API v1 ZedToken message. ZedTokens are returned by SpiceDB after each write operation and can be stored and supplied back in subsequent read operations to establish causality guarantees. They are used as AtLeastAsFresh to ensure reads reflect at least a known write, or as AtExactSnapshot to read at a precise revision. The raw token string is also surfaced by OnWatermarkFunc after each batch is committed. Use the static Create(string) factory method to construct a new instance. Properties Token Gets or sets the opaque token string returned by SpiceDB that encodes the snapshot revision at which a write was committed. string Token { get; set; } Property Value string Methods Create(string) Creates a new ISpiceDbZedToken instance from the specified token string. public static ISpiceDbZedToken Create(string token) Parameters token string The opaque token string returned by SpiceDB after a write operation, encoding the snapshot revision to use for causality. Returns ISpiceDbZedToken A new ISpiceDbZedToken with the specified token string.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.title: Enum SpiceDbPreconditionOperation | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.summary: Enum SpiceDbPreconditionOperation Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Specifies how the existence or absence of relationships matching a filter determines whether a SpiceDB write operation proceeds. public enum SpiceDbPreconditionOperation Fields MustMatch = 2 The parent request will fail if no relationships match the associated filter. MustNotMatch = 1 The parent request will fail if any relationships match the associated filter. Unspecified = 0 The operation is unspecified. This value must not be used in a precondition.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.title: Enum SpiceDbRelationshipUpdateOperation | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.summary: Enum SpiceDbRelationshipUpdateOperation Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Specifies the mutation operation to perform on a relationship in a SpiceDB write request. public enum SpiceDbRelationshipUpdateOperation Fields Create = 1 Creates the relationship only if it does not already exist. The request will fail if the relationship already exists. Delete = 3 Deletes the relationship. If the relationship does not exist, this operation will no-op. Touch = 2 Creates the relationship if it does not exist, or updates it if it already exists (upsert). The request will not fail if the relationship already exists. Unspecified = 0 The operation is unspecified. This value must not be used in a relationship update. Remarks This enum wraps the Authzed API v1 RelationshipUpdate.Operation type. It is set via Operation to control how the accompanying relationship is persisted when the request is committed.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.title: Class SpiceDbSinkOptions | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.summary: Class SpiceDbSinkOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Provides configuration options for the SpiceDB relationship sink connector. public class SpiceDbSinkOptions Inheritance object SpiceDbSinkOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Pass an instance of this class to SpiceDbConnectorManagerExtensions.AddSpiceDbSink to configure the gRPC connection, write behaviour, and lifecycle callbacks for the SpiceDB sink. Properties BatchSize Gets or sets the maximum number of relationship mutations to include in a single WriteRelationships request. Defaults to 50. public int BatchSize { get; set; } Property Value int BeforeWriteRequestFunc Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback invoked before each ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest is sent to SpiceDB. public Func<ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest, Task>? BeforeWriteRequestFunc { get; set; } Property Value Func<ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest, Task> Remarks Use this callback to inspect or modify the write request before it is submitted, for example to add preconditions or adjust relationship updates. Channel Gets or sets the gRPC channel used to connect to the SpiceDB API. public required ChannelBase Channel { get; set; } Property Value ChannelBase DeleteExistingDataFilter Gets or sets an optional filter that scopes which existing SpiceDB relationships are candidates for deletion after the initial data load has completed. public ISpiceDbReadRelationshipsRequest? DeleteExistingDataFilter { get; set; } Property Value ISpiceDbReadRelationshipsRequest Remarks When set, the sink reads all relationships from SpiceDB that match this filter after the initial data load. A relationship is deleted only if it satisfies both conditions: it matches this filter and it is not present in the result set. Relationships that do not match the filter are never deleted, regardless of whether they appear in the result set. ExecutionMode Gets or sets the execution mode for the sink operator. Defaults to Hybrid. public ExecutionMode ExecutionMode { get; set; } Property Value ExecutionMode GetMetadata Gets or sets an optional factory function that returns gRPC Grpc.Core.Metadata to attach to each request, such as an Authorization bearer token header. public Func<Metadata>? GetMetadata { get; set; } Property Value Func<Metadata> MaxParallellCalls Gets or sets the maximum number of WriteRelationships requests that may be in flight concurrently. Defaults to 4. public int MaxParallellCalls { get; set; } Property Value int OnInitialDataSentFunc Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback invoked once the initial data load has been fully committed to SpiceDB. public Func<Task>? OnInitialDataSentFunc { get; set; } Property Value Func<Task> Remarks Use this callback to perform post-load cleanup. For example, if an external store tracks tuples managed by this integration, it can delete any stale tuples that were not present in the initial result set. OnWatermarkFunc Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback invoked each time a new watermark is received after a successful batch write. public Func<Watermark, string, Task>? OnWatermarkFunc { get; set; } Property Value Func<Watermark, string, Task> Remarks The first argument is the Watermark from the stream. The second argument is the Token string returned by SpiceDB for the last committed write. Use this to track which source data has been successfully written to SpiceDB.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.summary: Class SpiceDbSourceOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Provides configuration options for the SpiceDB relationship source connector. public class SpiceDbSourceOptions Inheritance object SpiceDbSourceOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Pass an instance of this class to SpiceDbConnectorManagerExtensions.AddSpiceDbSource to configure the gRPC connection, request metadata, and read consistency for the SpiceDB source. The source performs an initial load of relationships via the ReadRelationships RPC and then streams ongoing changes using the SpiceDB Watch API. This setting is not applied to the Watch stream used for ongoing change detection after the initial load. Use ISpiceDbConsistency factory methods to specify an explicit requirement. GetMetadata Gets or sets an optional factory function that returns gRPC Grpc.Core.Metadata to attach to each request, such as an Authorization bearer token header. public Func<Metadata>? GetMetadata { get; set; } Property Value Func<Metadata>

api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.title: Class SpiceDbToFlowtide | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.Spi.summary: Class SpiceDbToFlowtide Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.dll Provides a utility method for converting a SpiceDB permission schema into a Flowtide query plan that computes the effective subjects for a given resource type and relation. public static class SpiceDbToFlowtide Inheritance object SpiceDbToFlowtide Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The generated Plan reads SpiceDB relationship data from a named Flowtide source table and applies the permission graph rules defined in the schema to resolve which subjects have access to which resources. Register the returned plan as a queryable view using AddPlanAsView and then reference it in SQL to obtain the expanded permission set. Methods Convert(string, string, string, string, bool, params string[]?) Converts a SpiceDB schema into a Flowtide Plan that, when executed, produces the effective set of subjects that have the specified relation on the given resource type. public static Plan Convert(string schemaText, string type, string relation, string inputTypeName, bool recurseAtStopType = false, params string[]? stopAtTypes) Parameters schemaText string The SpiceDB schema DSL text to parse, defining the resource types, relations, and permission rules. type string The resource type to compute effective permission membership for (for example, "document"). Must be defined in the schema. relation string The relation or permission name to expand on the resource type (for example, "view"). Must exist on the resource type specified by type. inputTypeName string The name of the Flowtide table that contains the SpiceDB relationship data, matching the view or table registered with the SpiceDB source connector. recurseAtStopType bool When true, the permission graph is expanded through the stop types themselves before halting. When false (the default), expansion stops at the boundary with stop types without recursing into them. stopAtTypes string[] An optional set of resource type names at which recursive expansion should stop. Useful for bounding expansion of deeply nested permission hierarchies. Every type listed must be defined in the schema. Returns Plan A Plan containing the Substrait relations that compute the effective permission set for the specified type and relation. Remarks The root relation of the returned plan produces rows with the following columns: subject_type, subject_id, subject_relation, relation, resource_type, and resource_id. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when type or any type in stopAtTypes is not defined in the schema. InvalidOperationException Thrown when relation does not exist on the resource type specified by type. ConvertAsync(ChannelBase, Metadata, string, string, string, bool, params string[]?) Reads the SpiceDB schema from the provided gRPC channel and converts a specific permission relation into a Flowtide plan using the Convert(string, string, string, string, bool, params string[]?) method. public static Task<Plan> ConvertAsync(ChannelBase channel, Metadata headers, string type, string relation, string inputTypeName, bool recurseAtStopType = false, params string[]? stopAtTypes) Parameters channel ChannelBase headers Metadata type string relation string inputTypeName string recurseAtStopType bool stopAtTypes string[] Returns Task<Plan>

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Compute.Columnar.summary: Interface ITableFunctionOutput Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Compute.Columnar Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll Sink that a compiled table function appends its generated rows into. public interface ITableFunctionOutput Properties Columns The function's output columns, one per column in the table function schema. For each produced row the function writes one value to every column and then calls CommitRow(int, uint). IReadOnlyList<IColumn> Columns { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<IColumn> Count Number of rows committed so far. int Count { get; } Property Value int Methods CommitRow(int, uint) Commits a produced row with the given weight and iteration. Must be called once, after the row's values have been written to Columns. void CommitRow(int weight, uint iteration) Parameters weight int iteration uint CommitRows(int, int, uint) Commits count produced rows that all share the same weight and iteration, after their values have been written to Columns. This lets a function that emits a run of uniform rows (e.g. unnesting a list) fill the bookkeeping in bulk instead of one CommitRow(int, uint) call per row. void CommitRows(int count, int weight, uint iteration) Parameters count int weight int iteration uint

First CanHandle is called, if the factory responds true, then ModifyPlan is called. After that, CanHandle is called again, if it returns true, then CreateSource is called. This is done since the plan may be modified to include multiple read relations in case of partitions. api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distribut.title: Class DistributedFlowtideStream | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distribut.summary: Class DistributedFlowtideStream Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distributed Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll A distributed stream where all substreams run in the same process. Built with DistributedStreamBuilder. public sealed class DistributedFlowtideStream : IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object DistributedFlowtideStream Implements IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Health The worst health across all substreams. The stream only processes data correctly when every substream is healthy, per substream details are available through Substreams. public FlowtideHealth Health { get; } Property Value FlowtideHealth StreamName public string StreamName { get; } Property Value string Substreams The substreams by substream name. public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, DataflowStream> Substreams { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyDictionary<string, DataflowStream> Methods DeleteAsync() Deletes the state of all substreams. public Task DeleteAsync() Returns Task DisposeAsync() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources asynchronously. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task that represents the asynchronous dispose operation. Pause() Pauses all substreams, they stop emitting data to their sinks until Resume() is called. Data exchanged between the substreams pauses with them since it is produced by the paused operators. public void Pause() Resume() Resumes all substreams after Pause(). public void Resume() StartAsync() Starts all substreams and drives their schedulers (no need to call RunAsync). A substream failure does not fail this call, it is retried in the background and surfaces through the failure listeners and Health. If the start itself throws, the substreams that did start are stopped before rethrowing. public Task StartAsync() Returns Task StopAsync() Stops all substreams. The substreams are stopped in parallel since a final checkpoint requires communication between the substreams. public Task StopAsync() Returns Task

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distribut.title: Class DistributedStreamBuilder | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distribut.summary: Class DistributedStreamBuilder Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distributed Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll Builds a distributed stream where all substreams run in the same process. The plan may already contain substreams (e.g. from SQL SUBSTREAM) or be split with DistributeAutomatically(int, Func<int, string>?); each substream runs as its own stream with its own storage and they communicate through a LocalSubstreamCommunicationHub. public class DistributedStreamBuilder Inheritance object DistributedStreamBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DistributedStreamBuilder(string) public DistributedStreamBuilder(string streamName) Parameters streamName string Methods AddConnectorManager(Func<string, string, IConnectorManager>) Sets the factory that creates the connector manager per substream (called with the stream and substream name). Each substream gets its own so connectors holding per-stream state, like sinks capturing callbacks, are never shared. public DistributedStreamBuilder AddConnectorManager(Func<string, string, IConnectorManager> connectorManagerFactory) Parameters connectorManagerFactory Func<string, string, IConnectorManager> Returns DistributedStreamBuilder AddPlan(Func<Plan>, bool, PlanOptimizerSettings?) Adds the factory that creates the plan to run. Called once per substream (building a stream mutates the plan in place), so it must be deterministic and return an identical plan every call, e.g. by rebuilding from the same SQL, or the substreams cannot communicate. public DistributedStreamBuilder AddPlan(Func<Plan> planFactory, bool optimize = true, PlanOptimizerSettings? planOptimizerSettings = null) Parameters planFactory Func<Plan> Factory that creates a new identical plan on every call. optimize bool If true each plan is optimized before it is handed to the substream. When false together with DistributeAutomatically(int, Func<int, string>?) the factory must return an already optimized plan, only merge joins can be partitioned. planOptimizerSettings PlanOptimizerSettings Optional optimizer settings. Returns DistributedStreamBuilder Build() public DistributedFlowtideStream Build() Returns DistributedFlowtideStream ConfigureSubstream(Action<string, FlowtideBuilder>) Optional hook to configure each substream builder, called with the substream name. Can be used to set listeners, schedulers or override any other setting per substream. public DistributedStreamBuilder ConfigureSubstream(Action<string, FlowtideBuilder> configureSubstream) Parameters configureSubstream Action<string, FlowtideBuilder> Returns DistributedStreamBuilder DistributeAutomatically(int, Func<int, string>?) Splits a plan that does not yet contain substreams into the given number of substreams using DistributedPlanModifier. public DistributedStreamBuilder DistributeAutomatically(int substreamCount, Func<int, string>? substreamNameGenerator = null) Parameters substreamCount int substreamNameGenerator Func<int, string> Returns DistributedStreamBuilder WithLoggerFactory(ILoggerFactory) public DistributedStreamBuilder WithLoggerFactory(ILoggerFactory loggerFactory) Parameters loggerFactory ILoggerFactory Returns DistributedStreamBuilder WithStateOptionsFactory(Func<string, string, StateManagerOptions>) Sets the factory that creates state manager options per substream (called with the stream and substream name). Storage must be keyed on both names: substream names repeat between streams and would collide if keyed on the substream name alone. public DistributedStreamBuilder WithStateOptionsFactory(Func<string, string, StateManagerOptions> stateOptionsFactory) Parameters stateOptionsFactory Func<string, string, StateManagerOptions> Returns DistributedStreamBuilder

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Lineage.OpenLine.title: Class OpenLineageHttpOptions | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Lineage.OpenLine.summary: Class OpenLineageHttpOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Lineage Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll Configuration options for reporting OpenLineage events over HTTP. public class OpenLineageHttpOptions Inheritance object OpenLineageHttpOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks These options are used by the OpenLineage HTTP reporter to send lineage events to an OpenLineage-compatible endpoint. Use Url to specify the target endpoint and OnRequest to customize outgoing HTTP requests (for example, to add authentication headers). The options can be configured through FlowtideBuilder.WithOpenLineageHttp or the IFlowtideDIBuilder.AddOpenLineageHttp extension method. Properties IncludeSchema Gets or sets a value indicating whether schema information should be included in the lineage events. public bool IncludeSchema { get; set; } Property Value bool OnRequest Gets or sets an optional callback that is invoked on each outgoing HttpRequestMessage before it is sent to the OpenLineage endpoint. public Action<HttpRequestMessage>? OnRequest { get; set; } Property Value Action<HttpRequestMessage> Remarks Use this to modify the HTTP request, for example to add custom headers such as authorization tokens or API keys. RunId Gets or sets an optional run identifier for the lineage events. public Guid? RunId { get; set; } Property Value Guid? Remarks When set, this Guid is used as the run ID for all lineage events emitted by the reporter. If null, a new Guid is generated automatically. Url Gets or sets the URL of the OpenLineage HTTP endpoint to send lineage events to. public string? Url { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks This property is required and must be set before the reporter is started. An ArgumentException is thrown at initialization if this value is null.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchan.title: Interface ISubstreamCommunicationHandler | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchan.summary: Interface ISubstreamCommunicationHandler Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchange Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll public interface ISubstreamCommunicationHandler Methods FetchData(IReadOnlySet<int>, int, CancellationToken) Fetches events from the other substream. Task<IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>> FetchData(IReadOnlySet<int> targetIds, int numberOfEvents, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters targetIds IReadOnlySet<int> The exchange targets to fetch from. numberOfEvents int Max number of events to fetch in total. cancellationToken CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>> Initialize(Func<IReadOnlySet<int>, int, CancellationToken, Task<IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>>>, Func<long, Task>, Func<long, long, bool, Task<SubstreamInitializeResponse>>, Func<long, long, bool, Task>) Registers the local callbacks that are invoked when the other substream sends requests to this substream. void Initialize(Func<IReadOnlySet<int>, int, CancellationToken, Task<IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>>> getDataFunction, Func<long, Task> callFailAndRecover, Func<long, long, bool, Task<SubstreamInitializeResponse>> initializeFromTarget, Func<long, long, bool, Task> callRecieveCheckpointDone) Parameters getDataFunction Func<IReadOnlySet<int>, int, CancellationToken, Task<IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>>> Returns events for the given exchange targets. callFailAndRecover Func<long, Task> Fails and recovers this substream to the given version. initializeFromTarget Func<long, long, bool, Task<SubstreamInitializeResponse>> Handles an initialize request from the other substream: the restore point, the requestors checkpoint epoch, and whether the requestor resumes from a clean handoff stop. callRecieveCheckpointDone Func<long, long, bool, Task> Handles a checkpoint done message with the completed checkpoint version, the epoch it was sent under, and whether the committed cycle covers consuming this substreams stop barriers. OnStreamFailure() Called when the local stream fails, before rollback and restart. Handlers that fetch destructively over a network change their fetch epoch here so pre-failure fetches are recognized as stale and refused. In-process handlers can ignore this. void OnStreamFailure() SendCheckpointDone(long, long, bool) Notifies the other substream that a checkpoint has completed in this substream. Task SendCheckpointDone(long checkpointVersion, long targetCheckpointEpoch, bool coversPeerStopBarrier) Parameters checkpointVersion long The completed checkpoint version. targetCheckpointEpoch long The receiving substream's checkpoint epoch as last learned through the handshake, so it can drop the ack if it is stale. coversPeerStopBarrier bool True when the committed cycle covers consuming the receiving substreams stop barriers, confirming a stop drain. Returns Task SendFailAndRecover(long) Task SendFailAndRecover(long restoreVersion) Parameters restoreVersion long Returns Task SendInitializeRequest(long, long, bool, CancellationToken) Runs the initialize handshake against the other substream. api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchan.title: Class SubstreamEventWireSerializer | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchan.summary: Class SubstreamEventWireSerializer Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchange Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll Wire format for events fetched between substreams on different nodes. The payload is a single opaque byte stream, so any transport (Orleans grain calls, an actor framework) can move it, using the same columnar encoding the exchange queues spill with (no per-row work). Serialize writes into an IBufferWriter<T> and Deserialize reads from a ReadOnlySequence<T>, so pooled segmented buffers (e.g. Orleans PooledBuffer) move events without intermediate byte arrays. Serialize never allocates batch memory; Deserialize allocates from the target's resolved allocator and references nothing in the payload, so the transport may release it once Deserialize returns. Rent ownership: the receiver claim from the dequeue is released by ReturnEvents(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>) on the sending side after serializing, and deserialized events carry a fresh claim, so the fetching side sees the same contract as a local fetch. public sealed class SubstreamEventWireSerializer Inheritance object SubstreamEventWireSerializer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Deserialize(in ReadOnlySequence<byte>, Func<int, IMemoryAllocator>) Deserializes a payload created by Serialize(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>, IBufferWriter<byte>). Batch memory is allocated from the allocator the resolver returns for the events exchange target id. Each rentable event is rented once, the receiver claim, matching what a local fetch hands out, the reader returns it after the event has been sent into the stream. public List<SubstreamEventData> Deserialize(in ReadOnlySequence<byte> payload, Func<int, IMemoryAllocator> allocatorResolver) Parameters payload ReadOnlySequence<byte> allocatorResolver Func<int, IMemoryAllocator> Returns List<SubstreamEventData> ReturnEvents(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>) Releases the receiver claims on events after they have been serialized, the local copies end their journey on the sending side, the receiving side works with the deserialized copies. public static void ReturnEvents(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> events) Parameters events IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> Serialize(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData>, IBufferWriter<byte>) Serializes fetched events into the buffer writer. Format: [int count] then per event [int exchangeTargetId][event]. public void Serialize(IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> events, IBufferWriter<byte> writer) Parameters events IReadOnlyList<SubstreamEventData> writer IBufferWriter<byte>

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchan.title: Class SubstreamInitializeResponse | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchan.summary: Class SubstreamInitializeResponse Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchange Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll public class SubstreamInitializeResponse Inheritance object SubstreamInitializeResponse Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors SubstreamInitializeResponse(bool, bool, long, long, long, bool) public SubstreamInitializeResponse(bool notStarted, bool success, long restoreVersion, long checkpointEpoch = 0, long recordedCheckpointEpoch = 0, bool cleanReconnect = false) Parameters notStarted bool success bool restoreVersion long checkpointEpoch long recordedCheckpointEpoch long cleanReconnect bool Properties CheckpointEpoch The responding substream's current checkpoint epoch. The requester records it as the peer epoch and tags its checkpoint done acks with it, so a stale ack from before a restart carries an old epoch and is dropped by the receiver. public long CheckpointEpoch { get; } Property Value long CleanReconnect The responder accepted the requestors clean handoff reconnect and kept running. The requestor then completes startup from restored state instead of waiting for a restart. public bool CleanReconnect { get; } Property Value bool NotStarted public bool NotStarted { get; } Property Value bool RecordedCheckpointEpoch The checkpoint epoch the responder has recorded for the REQUESTOR after handling this handshake (its record is highest-wins). Epochs are clock-seeded per process, so after a hard fail over onto a process whose clock seed is behind, a live requestor announces a lower epoch than its dead predecessor and the responder keeps the dead record - every ack the responder sends is then tagged with it and dropped by the requestor. When this value is higher than the epoch the requestor announced, it re-seeds above it and re-runs the handshake (see SubstreamCommunicationPoint.SendInitializeRequest) instead of being permanently fenced out of its acks. public long RecordedCheckpointEpoch { get; } Property Value long RestoreVersion public long RestoreVersion { get; } Property Value long Success public bool Success { get; } Property Value bool

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.Distri.title: Class DistributedPlanModifier | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.Distri.summary: Class DistributedPlanModifier Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll Splits a not-yet-distributed plan into substreams. Sink roots are assigned round robin, and partitionable operators (merge joins, aggregates, window functions) are split into one copy per substream by FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode.DistributeOperatorsVisitor. Existing exchange relations (e.g. from distributed views) are placed in one consumer's substream; consumers in other substreams get cross-substream targets and references. Requires an optimized plan (only merge joins can be partitioned) and runs as the last optimizer step when DistributedPlanOptions is set. Deterministic, so each substream host can compute the plan independently. public static class DistributedPlanModifier Inheritance object DistributedPlanModifier Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods ToDistributedPlan(Plan, DistributedPlanOptions) public static Plan ToDistributedPlan(Plan plan, DistributedPlanOptions options) Parameters plan Plan options DistributedPlanOptions Returns Plan

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.Distri.summary: Class DistributedPlanOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Core.dll Options for DistributedPlanModifier that controls how a plan is split into substreams. public class DistributedPlanOptions Inheritance object DistributedPlanOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties SubstreamCount Number of substreams to split the plan into. Partitionable operators (joins, aggregates, window functions) get one copy per substream, so even a single-sink plan uses them all; sink roots are assigned round robin. public required int SubstreamCount { get; init; } Property Value int SubstreamNameGenerator Optional generator for substream names, gets the substream index as input. Defaults to "substream_{index}". public Func<int, string>? SubstreamNameGenerator { get; init; } Property Value Func<int, string>

api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.Distri.title: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.Distri.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode Classes DistributedPlanModifier Splits a not-yet-distributed plan into substreams. Sink roots are assigned round robin, and partitionable operators (merge joins, aggregates, window functions) are split into one copy per substream by FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode.DistributeOperatorsVisitor. Existing exchange relations (e.g. from distributed views) are placed in one consumer's substream; consumers in other substreams get cross-substream targets and references. Requires an optimized plan (only merge joins can be partitioned) and runs as the last optimizer step when DistributedPlanOptions is set. Deterministic, so each substream host can compute the plan independently. DistributedPlanOptions Options for DistributedPlanModifier that controls how a plan is split into substreams.

Use "*" to match all table names. spiceDbSinkOptions SpiceDbSinkOptions The SpiceDbSinkOptions that configure the SpiceDB gRPC channel, batch size, write hooks, watermark callbacks, and the optional deletion filter applied after the initial data load. Returns IConnectorManager The same IConnectorManager instance to allow method chaining. Remarks The sink maps incoming rows to SpiceDB relationships using the columns resource_type, resource_id, relation, subject_type, subject_id, and subject_relation. Use "*" as regexPattern to match all table names. AddSpiceDbSource(IConnectorManager, string, SpiceDbSourceOptions) Registers a SpiceDB relationship source with the connector manager that reads relationships from tables whose names match the specified pattern. public static IConnectorManager AddSpiceDbSource(this IConnectorManager connectorManager, string regexPattern, SpiceDbSourceOptions spiceDbSourceOptions) Parameters connectorManager IConnectorManager The IConnectorManager to register the SpiceDB source with. regexPattern string A regular expression pattern used to match table names declared in the stream plan. Use "*" to match all table names. spiceDbSourceOptions SpiceDbSourceOptions The SpiceDbSourceOptions that configure the SpiceDB gRPC channel, request metadata, and read consistency requirements. Returns IConnectorManager The same IConnectorManager instance to allow method chaining. Remarks The source streams SpiceDB relationships as rows with columns resource_type, resource_id, relation, subject_type, subject_id, and subject_relation. Use "*" as regexPattern to match all table names.

Cannot be null. Returns IReservoirBuilder The same storage builder instance, configured to use Azure Blob Storage for persistent storage. Remarks Use this method to enable Flowtide to store data in Azure Blob Storage, with optional local disk caching and customizable storage options. Adds Azure Blob Storage as a storage provider using the provided connection string, container name, and optional directory path. public static IReservoirBuilder AddAzureBlobStorage(this IFlowtideStorageBuilder storageBuilder, string connectionString, string containerName, string? directoryPath = null, string? localCacheDirectory = null) Parameters storageBuilder IFlowtideStorageBuilder The storage builder to which Azure Blob Storage will be added. connectionString string The connection string used to authenticate and connect to the Azure Blob Storage account. Cannot be null or empty. containerName string The name of the Azure Blob Storage container to use. Cannot be null or empty. directoryPath string An optional directory path within the container where data will be stored. If not specified, the root of the container is used. localCacheDirectory string An optional local directory path for caching data. If not specified, the root of the container is used. localCacheDirectory string An optional local directory path for caching data.
