api/FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrateg.title: Class CustomExceptionStrategy | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrateg.summary: Class CustomExceptionStrategy Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrategies Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll An IFailureListener that delegates stream failure notifications to a caller-supplied Action<T> callback. public class CustomExceptionStrategy : IFailureListener Inheritance object CustomExceptionStrategy Implements IFailureListener Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Register this strategy via AddFailureListener(IFailureListener) or use FlowtideBuilder.WithFailureListener(Action<Exception?>), which constructs a CustomExceptionStrategy internally, to react to stream failures without implementing the full IFailureListener interface. Unlike ExitProcessStrategy and KillProcessStrategy, this strategy does not terminate the process. After the callback returns, the stream's built-in automatic recovery sequence continues normally, restarting the stream from the last committed checkpoint. Typical uses for the callback include custom logging, sending an alert, incrementing a failure metric, or signalling an external monitoring system. Constructors CustomExceptionStrategy(Action<Exception?>) Initializes a new CustomExceptionStrategy with the specified failure callback. public CustomExceptionStrategy(Action<Exception?> handler) Parameters handler Action<Exception> The Action<T> to invoke when a stream failure occurs. Receives the Exception that caused the failure, or null if no specific exception was captured at the point of failure. Methods OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) Invokes the callback supplied at construction time, passing the exception that caused the stream failure. public void OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamFailureNotification The StreamFailureNotification describing the stream failure. Only Exception is forwarded to the callback; StreamName is available if a custom IFailureListener implementation is preferred over this strategy. Remarks The callback receives Exception, which may be null if no specific exception was captured. After the callback returns, the stream's built-in automatic recovery proceeds normally; the callback must not block indefinitely, as this method is invoked synchronously within the failure transition.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrateg.title: Class ExitProcessStrategy | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrateg.summary: Class ExitProcessStrategy Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrategies Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll An IFailureListener that terminates the current process gracefully when the stream encounters an unhandled failure. public class ExitProcessStrategy : IFailureListener Inheritance object ExitProcessStrategy Implements IFailureListener Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Register this strategy via AddFailureListener(IFailureListener) or FlowtideBuilder.WithFailureListener to let a container orchestrator such as Kubernetes or Docker restart the process when the stream fails, rather than allowing the stream to attempt its built-in automatic recovery. OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) calls Exit(int) with the current ExitCode, which initiates a graceful shutdown: registered AppDomain.ProcessExit handlers and object finalizers are executed before the process terminates. For an immediate, non-graceful termination that skips cleanup, use KillProcessStrategy instead. Methods OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) Terminates the current process gracefully by calling Exit(int) with ExitCode. public void OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamFailureNotification The StreamFailureNotification describing the stream failure. Not used by this implementation. Remarks This method does not return. The process exits after running any registered AppDomain.ProcessExit event handlers and object finalizers. The contents of notification are not used by this implementation.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrateg.title: Class KillProcessStrategy | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrateg.summary: Class KillProcessStrategy Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrategies Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Base.dll An IFailureListener that immediately terminates the current process when the stream encounters an unhandled failure. public class KillProcessStrategy : IFailureListener Inheritance object KillProcessStrategy Implements IFailureListener Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Register this strategy via AddFailureListener(IFailureListener) or FlowtideBuilder.WithFailureListener to force a hard process termination when the stream fails, bypassing the stream's built-in automatic recovery and any registered shutdown handlers. This ensures that a container orchestrator such as Kubernetes or Docker always observes a non-zero process exit, triggering a clean container restart even if threads are stuck or cleanup code would otherwise hang. OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) calls Kill() on the current process, which terminates immediately without running AppDomain.ProcessExit event handlers, object finalizers, or any other cleanup code. For a graceful shutdown that allows registered cleanup handlers to run before the process exits, use ExitProcessStrategy instead. Methods OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) Immediately terminates the current process by calling Kill() on GetCurrentProcess(). public void OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamFailureNotification The StreamFailureNotification describing the stream failure. Not used by this implementation. Remarks This method does not return. The process is terminated immediately without running AppDomain.ProcessExit event handlers, object finalizers, or any other cleanup code. The contents of notification are not used by this implementation.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.AppendTree.IA.summary: Interface IAppendTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.AppendTree Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll A forward, read-only cursor over the entries of an IAppendTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer>. Seek(in K, in IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer>?) must be called to position the cursor before enumerating; iterating without seeking first throws. Once positioned, enumerate the iterator to walk entries in ascending key order from the seek position onward. public interface IAppendTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer> : IAsyncEnumerable<KeyValuePair<K, V>>, IDisposable where TKeyContainer : IKeyContainer<K> Type Parameters K The key type of the tree. V The value type of the tree. TKeyContainer The container used to store keys within a node. Inherited Members IAsyncEnumerable<KeyValuePair<K, V>>.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken) IDisposable.Dispose() Methods Seek(in K, in IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer>?) Positions the cursor at the first entry whose key is greater than or equal to key. Must be called before the iterator is enumerated. ValueTask Seek(in K key, in IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer>? searchComparer = null) Parameters key K The key to seek to. searchComparer IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer> An optional comparer used to locate the key, for example to seek on a partial key. When null the tree's default key comparer is used. Returns ValueTask

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructure.summary: Class BitmapList Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll A growable list of bits packed into 32-bit words and backed by unmanaged memory allocated from an IMemoryAllocator. It supports the usual list operations (add, get, set, insert and remove) as well as range and batch variants that shift large blocks of bits at once, using vectorized shifts where available. It is commonly used as a validity (null) mask for columnar data. Instances own native memory and must be disposed; Dispose() releases the buffer and returns the instance to FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures.BitmapListFactory for reuse. public class BitmapList : IReadOnlyList<bool>, IReadOnlyCollection<bool>, IEnumerable<bool>, IEnumerable, IDisposable Inheritance object BitmapList Implements IReadOnlyList<bool> IReadOnlyCollection<bool> IEnumerable<bool> IEnumerable IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors BitmapList() Creates an unassigned instance. Assign(IMemoryAllocator) must be called before use. Used together with pooling so an instance can be reused. public BitmapList() BitmapList(IMemoryAllocator) Creates an empty list that allocates backing memory from the given allocator on demand. public BitmapList(IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator) Parameters memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The allocator used for backing memory. BitmapList(IMemoryAllocator, int) Creates an empty list with backing memory pre-allocated for at least initialCapacity bits. public BitmapList(IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator, int initialCapacity) Parameters memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The allocator used for backing memory. initialCapacity int The number of bits to reserve capacity for up front. BitmapList(IMemoryOwner<byte>, int, IMemoryAllocator) Creates a list that wraps already populated memory, taking ownership of it. public BitmapList(IMemoryOwner<byte> memoryOwner, int length, IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator) Parameters memoryOwner IMemoryOwner<byte> The memory holding the packed bits. length int The number of valid bits in memoryOwner. memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The allocator used for any later growth. Properties Count The number of bits in the list. public int Count { get; } Property Value int this[int] Gets the bit at the given index. public bool this[int index] { get; } Parameters index int The zero based bit index. Property Value bool Memory The full backing memory buffer, including any capacity beyond the current bits, or empty when nothing is allocated. public Memory<byte> Memory { get; } Property Value Memory<byte> MemorySlice The portion of the backing memory that actually holds the current bits, rounded up to whole words. Use this when serializing or copying the bitmap. public Memory<byte> MemorySlice { get; } Property Value Memory<byte> Methods Add(bool) Appends a bit to the end of the list, growing it by one. public void Add(bool value) Parameters value bool The bit value to append. Assign(IMemoryAllocator) Resets the list to empty and assigns the allocator it should use, without allocating any memory yet. public void Assign(IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator) Parameters memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The allocator used for backing memory. Assign(IMemoryOwner<byte>, int, IMemoryAllocator) Resets the list to wrap already populated memory, taking ownership of it. Used together with pooling so an instance can be reused. public void Assign(IMemoryOwner<byte> memory, int length, IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator) Parameters memory IMemoryOwner<byte> The memory holding the packed bits. length int The number of valid bits in memory. memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The allocator used for any later growth. Clear() Resets the count to zero, keeping the backing memory for reuse. public void Clear() Copy(IMemoryAllocator) Creates a deep copy of the list, allocating new backing memory from the given allocator. public BitmapList Copy(IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator) Parameters memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The allocator used for the copy's backing memory. Returns BitmapList A new list with the same bits. CountFalseInRange(int, int) Counts how many bits are set to false in the range starting at index. public int CountFalseInRange(int index, int count) Parameters index int The zero based bit index to start counting from. count int The number of bits to examine. Returns int The number of false bits in the range. CountTrueInRange(int, int) Counts how many bits are set to true in the range starting at index. public int CountTrueInRange(int index, int count) Parameters index int The zero based bit index to start counting from. count int The number of bits to examine. Returns int The number of true bits in the range. DeleteBatch(ReadOnlySpan<int>) Batch delete bits at the specified sorted indices. This is more efficient than calling RemoveAt repeatedly because it processes contiguous blocks of retained bits in a single left-to-right sweep. public void DeleteBatch(ReadOnlySpan<int> targets) Parameters targets ReadOnlySpan<int> A span of sorted indices (ascending) of bits to delete. Returns int The index of the next false bit, or -1 if there is none. FindNextTrueIndex(int) Finds the index of the next true bit at or after start. public int FindNextTrueIndex(int start) Parameters start int The zero based index to start searching from. Returns int The index of the next true bit, or -1 if there is none. Get(int) Gets the bit at the given index, returning false when the index is outside the allocated range. public bool Get(int index) Parameters index int The zero based bit index. Returns bool GetByteSize(int, int) Returns the number of bytes needed to store the bits in the range as packed words. public int GetByteSize(int start, int end) Parameters start int The zero based index of the first bit in the range. end int The exclusive zero based index of the end of the range. api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructure.title: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructure.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures Classes BitmapList A growable list of bits packed into 32-bit words and backed by unmanaged memory allocated from an IMemoryAllocator. It supports the usual list operations (add, get, set, insert and remove) as well as range and batch variants that shift large blocks of bits at once, using vectorized shifts where available. It is commonly used as a validity (null) mask for columnar data. Instances own native memory and must be disposed; Dispose() releases the buffer and returns the instance to FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures.BitmapListFactory for reuse. PrimitiveList<T> A growable list of unmanaged values stored in native memory allocated from an IMemoryAllocator. It behaves like a list (add, insert, remove, index) but keeps its elements in a contiguous unmanaged buffer, which can be exposed as a Span or Memory and serialized without copying element by element. Ownership is reference counted through Rent(int) and Return(), and the buffer is released when the list is disposed or the last reference is returned.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.IAppendTree-4.title: Interface IAppendTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.IAppendTree-4.summary: Interface IAppendTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll A B+ tree that only supports appending entries in strictly increasing key order, which keeps all writes in the rightmost leaf and avoids the node splitting a general purpose tree needs. It is suited to time ordered data such as a time series, where new entries always have a larger key than everything already stored. Data is paged through a state client so the tree can grow beyond memory and be persisted across checkpoints. public interface IAppendTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> where TKeyContainer : IKeyContainer<K> Type Parameters K The key type, appended in increasing order. V The value type stored against each key. TKeyContainer The container used to store keys within a node. TValueContainer The container used to store values within a node. Methods Append(in K, in V) Appends an entry to the end of the tree. The key must be greater than the last appended key, otherwise an InvalidOperationException is thrown. ValueTask Append(in K key, in V value) Parameters key K The key to append, which must be larger than every key already in the tree. value V The value to store against key. Returns ValueTask Clear() Clears all data and resets the tree to an empty state. Useful for temporary storage that should be discarded on demand. ValueTask Clear() Returns ValueTask Commit() Persists pending changes through the underlying state client. ValueTask Commit() Returns ValueTask CreateIterator() Creates an iterator that can seek to a key and enumerate entries in ascending key order. IAppendTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer> CreateIterator() Returns IAppendTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer> Prune(K) Removes data from the start of the tree up to key. Whole leaf nodes are dropped without being loaded into memory; data within a partially covered leaf is trimmed in place. api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.R.title: Interface IReservoirBuilder | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.R.summary: Interface IReservoirBuilder Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Allows configuring the reservoir storage options, such as cache settings, snapshot intervals, and storage providers. This interface is used during the setup of the reservoir storage to specify how the reservoir should manage its data storage and caching behavior. public interface IReservoirBuilder Remarks The reservoir storage is designed to efficiently manage the state of streams by utilizing a combination of primary storage and optional caching. The options configured through this interface can have a significant impact on the performance and resource utilization of the reservoir storage. For example, enabling caching can improve read and write performance by reducing the number of interactions with the underlying storage provider. Methods DisableCache() Disables the use of a cache for the reservoir storage. void DisableCache() Remarks When this method is called, all read and write operations will interact directly with the underlying storage provider without utilizing an intermediate caching layer. This may result in increased latency for read and write operations, especially if the underlying storage provider has higher access times. Use this option when you want to ensure that all data is read from and written to the primary storage without any caching optimizations. Some storage providers will not work without the use of the local cache. OldStreamVersionsRetention(int) Sets the number of previous stream versions to retain in storage. Stream versioning is opt-in and requires explicit configuration on the stream builder using AddVersioningFromPlanHash(), AddVersioningFromString(), or AddVersioningFromAssembly(). Without versioning, the stream uses a single default version and this setting has no effect. The current version is always preserved; this setting controls how many previous versions are kept alongside it. Default is -1 (all versions are kept). void OldStreamVersionsRetention(int versionsToKeep) Parameters versionsToKeep int The number of previous stream versions to retain. A value of -1 retains all versions without limit. Remarks A value of -1 retains all versions indefinitely. A value of 0 deletes all old versions immediately after a checkpoint, keeping only the current version. A value of 1 keeps one previous version in addition to the current version. Versions are sorted by their last initialization time; the oldest versions beyond the retention count are deleted first. This controls cleanup of old stream versions, not old checkpoints within a version. SetCache(IReservoirStorageProvider) Sets the cache provider for the reservoir storage. The cache is used to store frequently accessed data in memory, which can improve performance by reducing the number of read and write operations to the underlying storage provider. void SetCache(IReservoirStorageProvider cache) Parameters cache IReservoirStorageProvider SetCacheSize(long) Sets the maximum size of the cache in bytes. When the cache exceeds this size, the least recently used items will be evicted. void SetCacheSize(long cacheSize) Parameters cacheSize long The maximum size of the cache in bytes. When the cache exceeds this size, the least recently used items will be evicted. SetMaxDataFileSize(int) Sets the maximum allowed file size for the reservoir storage, in bytes. This limits the size of the uploaded files to the underlying storage provider. void SetMaxDataFileSize(int maxFileSize) Parameters maxFileSize int The maximum allowed file size, in bytes. SetMemoryPool(MemoryPool<byte>) Sets the memory pool to be used for buffer management in the reservoir storage. void SetMemoryPool(MemoryPool<byte> memoryPool) Parameters memoryPool MemoryPool<byte> The memory pool to be used for buffer management in the reservoir storage. SetSnapshotCheckpointInterval(int) Sets the interval, in checkpoints, at which a snapshot checkpoint is created. A lower value results in more frequent snapshot checkpoints, which can improve recovery time at the cost of increased overhead due to more frequent snapshot creation. void SetSnapshotCheckpointInterval(int snapshotInterval) Parameters snapshotInterval int The interval, in checkpoints, at which a snapshot checkpoint is created. SetStorage(IReservoirStorageProvider) Sets the storage provider for the reservoir storage. The storage provider is responsible for handling the actual read and write operations to the underlying storage system, such as a file system, cloud storage, or database. void SetStorage(IReservoirStorageProvider reservoirStorageProvider) Parameters reservoirStorageProvider IReservoirStorageProvider The storage provider to be used for the reservoir storage.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.R.title: Class ReservoirBuilder | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.R.summary: Class ReservoirBuilder Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll public class ReservoirBuilder : IReservoirBuilder Inheritance object ReservoirBuilder Implements IReservoirBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Build() public ReservoirStorageOptions Build() Returns ReservoirStorageOptions DisableCache() Disables the use of a cache for the reservoir storage. public void DisableCache() Remarks When this method is called, all read and write operations will interact directly with the underlying storage provider without utilizing an intermediate caching layer. This may result in increased latency for read and write operations, especially if the underlying storage provider has higher access times. Use this option when you want to ensure that all data is read from and written to the primary storage without any caching optimizations. Some storage providers will not work without the use of the local cache. OldStreamVersionsRetention(int) Sets the number of previous stream versions to retain in storage. Stream versioning is opt-in and requires explicit configuration on the stream builder using AddVersioningFromPlanHash(), AddVersioningFromString(), or AddVersioningFromAssembly(). Without versioning, the stream uses a single default version and this setting has no effect. The current version is always preserved; this setting controls how many previous versions are kept alongside it. Default is -1 (all versions are kept). public void OldStreamVersionsRetention(int versionsToKeep) Parameters versionsToKeep int The number of previous stream versions to retain. A value of -1 retains all versions without limit. Remarks A value of -1 retains all versions indefinitely. A value of 0 deletes all old versions immediately after a checkpoint, keeping only the current version. A value of 1 keeps one previous version in addition to the current version. Versions are sorted by their last initialization time; the oldest versions beyond the retention count are deleted first. This controls cleanup of old stream versions, not old checkpoints within a version. SetCache(IReservoirStorageProvider) Sets the cache provider for the reservoir storage. The cache is used to store frequently accessed data in memory, which can improve performance by reducing the number of read and write operations to the underlying storage provider. public void SetCache(IReservoirStorageProvider cache) Parameters cache IReservoirStorageProvider SetCacheSize(long) Sets the maximum size of the cache in bytes. When the cache exceeds this size, the least recently used items will be evicted. public void SetCacheSize(long cacheSize) Parameters cacheSize long The maximum size of the cache in bytes. When the cache exceeds this size, the least recently used items will be evicted. SetMaxDataFileSize(int) Sets the maximum allowed file size for the reservoir storage, in bytes. This limits the size of the uploaded files to the underlying storage provider. public void SetMaxDataFileSize(int maxFileSize) Parameters maxFileSize int The maximum allowed file size, in bytes. SetMemoryPool(MemoryPool<byte>) Sets the memory pool to be used for buffer management in the reservoir storage. public void SetMemoryPool(MemoryPool<byte> memoryPool) Parameters memoryPool MemoryPool<byte> The memory pool to be used for buffer management in the reservoir storage. SetSnapshotCheckpointInterval(int) Sets the interval, in checkpoints, at which a snapshot checkpoint is created. A lower value results in more frequent snapshot checkpoints, which can improve recovery time at the cost of increased overhead due to more frequent snapshot creation. public void SetSnapshotCheckpointInterval(int snapshotInterval) Parameters snapshotInterval int The interval, in checkpoints, at which a snapshot checkpoint is created. SetStorage(IReservoirStorageProvider) Sets the storage provider for the reservoir storage. The storage provider is responsible for handling the actual read and write operations to the underlying storage system, such as a file system, cloud storage, or database. public void SetStorage(IReservoirStorageProvider reservoirStorageProvider) Parameters reservoirStorageProvider IReservoirStorageProvider The storage provider to be used for the reservoir storage.

This in relation to the max file size. For example, with a max file size of 64 MB and a compaction ratio threshold of 0.33, compaction will be triggered when active pages are below 21 MB. public float CompactionFileSizeRatioThreshold { get; set; } Property Value float FileProvider public IReservoirStorageProvider? FileProvider { get; set; } Property Value IReservoirStorageProvider KeepLastStreamVersions Gets or sets the number of previous stream versions to retain. Stream versioning is opt-in and requires explicit configuration on the stream builder using AddVersioningFromPlanHash(), AddVersioningFromString(), or AddVersioningFromAssembly(). Without versioning, the stream uses a single default version and this setting has no effect. The current version is always preserved; this setting controls how many previous versions are kept alongside it. public int KeepLastStreamVersions { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks A value of -1 retains all versions indefinitely (default). A value of 0 deletes all old versions immediately after a checkpoint, keeping only the current version. A value of 1 keeps one previous version in addition to the current version. Versions are sorted by their last initialization time; the oldest versions beyond the retention count are deleted first. This controls cleanup of old stream versions, not old checkpoints within a version. MaxCacheSizeBytes If Cache is configured, this sets the maximum size of the cache in bytes. When the cache exceeds this size, the least recently used items will be evicted. MemoryPool public MemoryPool<byte> MemoryPool { get; set; } Property Value MemoryPool<byte> SnapshotCheckpointInterval Gets or sets the interval, in operations, at which a snapshot checkpoint is created. public int SnapshotCheckpointInterval { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks A lower value results in more frequent snapshot checkpoints, which can improve recovery time but may impact performance due to increased snapshot creation. A higher value reduces the frequency of snapshot checkpoints, potentially increasing recovery time after a failure.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.R.title: Class StorageProviderContext | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.R.summary: Class StorageProviderContext Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Provides contextual information supplied to an IReservoirStorageProvider during initialization. public class StorageProviderContext Inheritance object StorageProviderContext Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks A StorageProviderContext is constructed by the storage system and passed to InitializeAsync(StorageProviderContext, CancellationToken) before the provider is used. Storage providers use the values it exposes to derive stream-specific storage paths and to allocate any native or pooled memory buffers they require at runtime. Constructors StorageProviderContext(string, string, IMemoryAllocator) Initializes a new instance of the StorageProviderContext class. public StorageProviderContext(string streamName, string streamVersion, IMemoryAllocator memoryAllocator) Parameters streamName string The logical name of the stream that owns the storage provider. streamVersion string The version string used to isolate this stream's data within the storage backend. This is typically derived from the stream's plan hash or a semantic version identifier. memoryAllocator IMemoryAllocator The memory allocator the storage provider should use to allocate native or pooled memory buffers. Properties MemoryAllocator Gets the memory allocator the storage provider should use for allocating native or pooled memory buffers. public IMemoryAllocator MemoryAllocator { get; } Property Value IMemoryAllocator Remarks Providers that perform direct I/O or manage native memory receive this allocator at initialization so that all memory operations remain consistent with the broader stream memory-management strategy. StreamName Gets the logical name of the stream that owns the storage provider. public string StreamName { get; } Property Value string Remarks Storage providers use this value to create or resolve stream-specific directories and file paths within their backing storage system. StreamVersion Gets the version string that identifies the specific version of the stream. public string StreamVersion { get; } Property Value string Remarks This value is typically a plan hash or a semantic version string derived from StreamVersionInformation. Storage providers use it to scope stored data to a particular stream version, enabling multiple incompatible plan versions to coexist in the same backend without interfering with each other.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.SerializableO.title: Struct SerializableObject | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.SerializableO.summary: Struct SerializableObject Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll A value handed to a file cache or persistent storage session to be written. It carries the data in one of two forms so callers do not have to serialize ahead of time: either a live ICacheObject together with the IStateSerializer that knows how to serialize it, or a block of bytes that has already been serialized. The bytes form is used when data is copied straight from the file cache to persistent storage, or for already serialized blobs such as metadata, where re-serialization is not needed. public struct SerializableObject Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors SerializableObject(ICacheObject, IStateSerializer) Creates a value that is serialized on demand, used when flushing a live object from the in-memory cache. public SerializableObject(ICacheObject obj, IStateSerializer serializer) Parameters obj ICacheObject The cache object to serialize when the value is written. serializer IStateSerializer The serializer that knows how to serialize obj. SerializableObject(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>) Creates a value from bytes that are already serialized, used when copying data straight from the file cache to persistent storage or when writing pre-serialized blobs such as metadata. public SerializableObject(ReadOnlyMemory<byte> serialized) Parameters serialized ReadOnlyMemory<byte> The serialized bytes to write. Properties HasPreSerializedData True when the value was created from already serialized bytes, meaning PreSerializedData can be written directly without invoking a serializer. public bool HasPreSerializedData { get; } Property Value bool PreSerializedData The already serialized bytes, or null when the value still has to be serialized through its IStateSerializer. Lets a session that can write raw bytes (for example pinning them for a native call) avoid going through Serialize(IBufferWriter<byte>). public ReadOnlyMemory<byte>? PreSerializedData { get; } Property Value ReadOnlyMemory<byte>? Methods Serialize(IBufferWriter<byte>) Writes the value to writer, either by copying the pre-serialized bytes or by running the serializer over the cache object. public void Serialize(IBufferWriter<byte> writer) Parameters writer IBufferWriter<byte> The buffer writer that receives the serialized bytes. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when the value was not constructed with either form of data.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateManager..summary: Class StateManagerOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateManager Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll public class StateManagerOptions Inheritance object StateManagerOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties CachePageCount public int CachePageCount { get; set; } Property Value int DefaultBPlusTreePageSize public int DefaultBPlusTreePageSize { get; set; } Property Value int DefaultBPlusTreePageSizeBytes public int DefaultBPlusTreePageSizeBytes { get; set; } Property Value int FileCacheFactory public IFileCacheFactory? FileCacheFactory { get; set; } Property Value IFileCacheFactory MaxProcessMemory Optional: Set a maximum process memory limit for the state manager. If the limit is reached, the state manager will start evicting pages from the cache. This can help keep the application at a steady memory usage, but can also cause performance issues if the limit is too low. Setting this value increases cache page count if memory usage is low. -1 disables the limit and only uses cache page count. public long MaxProcessMemory { get; set; } Property Value long MinCachePageCount The minimum cache page count, used to keep pages in memory to reduce latency. public int MinCachePageCount { get; set; } Property Value int PersistentStorage public IPersistentStorage? PersistentStorage { get; set; } Property Value IPersistentStorage SerializeOptions public StateSerializeOptions SerializeOptions { get; set; } Property Value StateSerializeOptions TemporaryStorageOptions Used if file cache factory is not set, with the default file cache public FileCacheOptions? TemporaryStorageOptions { get; set; } Property Value FileCacheOptions UseReadCache Uses temporary cache as a read cache. This can help increase performance and lower I/O against persistence storage. Useful if persistent storage is not on disk and instead in a remote location. public bool UseReadCache { get; set; } Property Value bool

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateSerializ.title: Class StateSerializeOptions | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateSerializ.summary: Class StateSerializeOptions Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Controls how state pages are serialized to the file cache and persistent storage, mainly how they are compressed. An instance left at its defaults applies no compression. Configure it through the storage builder, for example with ZstdPageCompression or NoCompression. public class StateSerializeOptions Inheritance object StateSerializeOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties CompressionLevel The compression level passed to the compressor. When null a default level of 3 is used. Only used when CompressionType enables compression. public int? CompressionLevel { get; set; } Property Value int? CompressionMethod The granularity at which compression is applied. Defaults to Page. public CompressionMethod CompressionMethod { get; set; } Property Value CompressionMethod CompressionType The compression algorithm to use. Defaults to None. Compression is only applied when this is set to Zstd together with Page. public CompressionType CompressionType { get; set; } Property Value CompressionType

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StreamVersion.title: Class StreamVersionInformation | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StreamVersion.summary: Class StreamVersionInformation Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Identifies the version of a running stream so that persisted state can be matched to the plan that produced it. It is supplied through the stream builder (SetVersionInformation) and flows into storage initialization, where providers use it to detect plan changes between restarts and to scope stored data to a particular version. public class StreamVersionInformation Inheritance object StreamVersionInformation Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors StreamVersionInformation(string, string) Creates version information for a stream. public StreamVersionInformation(string hash, string version) Parameters hash string A content hash of the stream plan, used to detect structural changes. version string A human-readable version label for the stream configuration. Properties Hash A content hash of the stream plan. On startup it is compared against the hash stored in persisted state; a mismatch indicates the stream topology has changed and is used to guard against incompatible state restores. public string Hash { get; } Property Value string Version A human-readable version label associated with the stream configuration. Storage providers can use it to scope stored data to a particular stream version. public string Version { get; } Property Value string

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTr.title: Interface IBPlusTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTr.summary: Interface IBPlusTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll A persistent B+ tree mapping keys to values. Keys and values are held in containers and the tree is paged through a state client, so it can grow beyond memory, offload to storage and be persisted across checkpoints. Reads and writes are asynchronous because a lookup may need to load pages from storage. public interface IBPlusTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> where TKeyContainer : IKeyContainer<K> where TValueContainer : IValueContainer<V> Type Parameters K The key type. V The value type. TKeyContainer The container used to store keys within a node. TValueContainer The container used to store values within a node. Properties CacheMisses The number of reads that missed the in-memory cache and had to load a page from storage. long CacheMisses { get; } Property Value long Methods Clear() Clears the tree and returns a blank new tree. This is useful for temporary data storage which will offload to disk when the size grows, but can be deleted on demand. ValueTask Clear() Returns ValueTask Commit() Persists pending changes through the underlying state client. ValueTask Commit() Returns ValueTask CreateBackwardIterator() Creates an iterator that walks the tree in descending key order. IBPlusTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CreateBackwardIterator() Returns IBPlusTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CreateBulkInserter() Creates an inserter that applies a batch of rows in one pass, more efficiently than inserting each row on its own. IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CreateBulkInserter() Returns IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CreateBulkSearcher<TComparer>(TComparer) Creates a searcher that looks up many keys in one pass across the leaves, using the given comparer. More efficient than calling GetValue(in K) per key for a large, sorted batch. IBplusTreeBulkSearch<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer, TComparer> CreateBulkSearcher<TComparer>(TComparer comparer) where TComparer : IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer> Parameters comparer TComparer The comparer used to locate the keys. Returns IBplusTreeBulkSearch<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer, TComparer> Type Parameters TComparer The comparer type used to locate the keys. CreateIterator() Creates an iterator that walks the tree in ascending key order. IBPlusTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CreateIterator() Returns IBPlusTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> Delete(in K) Removes the key from the tree if it is present. ValueTask Delete(in K key) Parameters key K The key to remove. Returns ValueTask GetKey(in K) Looks up the stored key that matches the given key. Useful when the stored key carries more data than the part used for lookup. ValueTask<(bool found, K? key)> GetKey(in K key) Parameters key K The key to look up. Returns ValueTask<(bool found, K key)> Whether a matching key was found and, if so, the stored key. GetValue(in K) Looks up the value stored for a key. ValueTask<(bool found, V? value)> GetValue(in K key) Parameters key K The key to look up. Returns ValueTask<(bool found, V value)> Whether the key was found and, if so, its value. RMW(in K, in V?, in GenericWriteFunction<V>) Performs a read-modify-write on the key by running function and applying the operation it returns, also returning the resulting value. ValueTask<(GenericWriteOperation operation, V? result)> RMW(in K key, in V? value, in GenericWriteFunction<V> function) Parameters key K The key to write. value V The value passed to function as its input. function GenericWriteFunction<V> The callback that decides the new value and the operation to apply. Returns ValueTask<(GenericWriteOperation operation, V result)> The operation that was applied and the resulting value. RMWNoResult(in K, in V?, in GenericWriteFunction<V>) Performs a read-modify-write on the key by running function and applying the operation it returns, without returning the resulting value. Cheaper than RMW(in K, in V?, in GenericWriteFunction<V>) when the result is not needed. ValueTask<GenericWriteOperation> RMWNoResult(in K key, in V? value, in GenericWriteFunction<V> function) Parameters key K The key to write. value V The value passed to function as its input. function GenericWriteFunction<V> The callback that decides the new value and the operation to apply. Returns ValueTask<GenericWriteOperation> The operation that was applied. Upsert(in K, in V) Inserts the key with the given value, or replaces the existing value if the key is already present. ValueTask Upsert(in K key, in V value) Parameters key K The key to insert or update. value V The value to store. Returns ValueTask

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTr.title: Interface IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTr.summary: Interface IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Applies a whole batch of rows to a B+ tree in a single pass. The batch is sorted by key and grouped by the leaf each row lands in, so each affected leaf is loaded once and any required node splits and merges are handled together. This is more efficient than upserting rows one at a time when writing a large batch. Each row's effect (insert, update, delete or no change) is decided by an IRowMutator<K, V>. public interface IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> where TKeyContainer : IKeyContainer<K> where TValueContainer : IValueContainer<V> Type Parameters K The key type. V The value type. TKeyContainer The container used to store keys within a node. TValueContainer The container used to store values within a node. Properties LeafHitCount The number of distinct leaves the most recent batch touched. int LeafHitCount { get; } Property Value int Methods ApplyBatch<TMutator>(K[], V[], int, int[], int[], TMutator, int) Applies a batch of rows to the tree using precomputed sorted indices and duplicate tags, avoiding an internal sort. ValueTask ApplyBatch<TMutator>(K[] keys, V[] values, int keyLength, int[] sortedIndices, int[] duplicateTags, TMutator mutator, int totalBatchByteSize) where TMutator : IRowMutator<K, V> Parameters keys K[] The keys of the batch. values V[] The values of the batch, aligned with keys. keyLength int The number of rows in the batch to use. sortedIndices int[] The indices of the rows in ascending key order, as produced by SortAndGetIndices(K[], int). duplicateTags int[] Per sorted row, a group id that is equal for rows with the same key. mutator TMutator The mutator that decides the operation for each row. totalBatchByteSize int The total serialized byte size of the batch, used to decide page splitting. Returns ValueTask Type Parameters TMutator The mutator type that decides each row's operation. ApplyBatch<TMutator>(K[], V[], int, TMutator, int) Applies a batch of rows to the tree, sorting the keys internally. ValueTask ApplyBatch<TMutator>(K[] keys, V[] values, int keyLength, TMutator mutator, int totalBatchByteSize) where TMutator : IRowMutator<K, V> Parameters keys K[] The keys of the batch. values V[] The values of the batch, aligned with keys. keyLength int The number of rows in the batch to use. mutator TMutator The mutator that decides the operation for each row. totalBatchByteSize int The total serialized byte size of the batch, used to decide page splitting. Returns ValueTask Type Parameters TMutator The mutator type that decides each row's operation. SortAndGetIndices(K[], int) Sorts the first keyLength keys and returns their indices in sorted order, without reordering the input array. The result can be passed to the overload of ApplyBatch<TMutator>(K[], V[], int, TMutator, int) that takes precomputed indices to avoid sorting again. The returned array is an internal buffer that is reused between calls. int[] SortAndGetIndices(K[] keys, int keyLength) Parameters keys K[] The keys to sort. keyLength int The number of entries in keys to use. Returns int[] The indices of the keys in ascending key order.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBplusTr.summary: Interface IBplusTreeBulkSearch<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer, TComparer> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Searches many keys against a B+ tree in a single pass. The keys are sorted and grouped by the leaf they fall in, so each leaf is loaded once and the results are produced one leaf at a time. After Start(K[], int), call MoveNextLeaf() in a loop and read CurrentLeaf and CurrentResults for each leaf until it returns false. A key whose match runs to the end of a leaf is carried over into the next leaf. Dispose the searcher to release the leaf page it holds. public interface IBplusTreeBulkSearch<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer, TComparer> : IDisposable where TKeyContainer : IKeyContainer<K> where TValueContainer : IValueContainer<V> where TComparer : IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer> Type Parameters K The key type. V The value type. TKeyContainer The container used to store keys within a node. TValueContainer The container used to store values within a node. TComparer The comparer used to locate the keys. Inherited Members IDisposable.Dispose() Properties CurrentLeaf The current leaf node. Only valid after MoveNextLeaf() returns true. LeafNode<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CurrentLeaf { get; } Property Value LeafNode<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> CurrentResults The search results for the current leaf. Each entry describes one search key and the index range it matched in the leaf. IReadOnlyList<BulkSearchKeyResult> CurrentResults { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<BulkSearchKeyResult> Methods MoveNextLeaf() Advances to the next leaf that has search keys mapped to it, exposing its matches through CurrentLeaf and CurrentResults. Returns false when all leaves have been visited. ValueTask<bool> MoveNextLeaf() Returns ValueTask<bool> True if a leaf with results is now current, false when the search is complete. Start(K[], int) Begins a search for a batch of keys, sorting them internally before routing them to the leaves. Returns ValueTask Start(K[], int, int[]) Begins a search for a batch of keys using precomputed sorted indices, avoiding an internal sort. ValueTask Start(K[] keys, int keyLength, int[] sortedIndices) Parameters keys K[] The keys to search for. keyLength int The number of entries in keys to use. sortedIndices int[] The indices of the keys in ascending key order. Returns ValueTask

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IKeyCont.summary: Interface IKeyContainer<K> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Stores the keys of a single B+ tree node. Implementations choose how keys are laid out, for example a managed list or a contiguous unmanaged buffer, and the tree uses this abstraction to search, insert, update and remove keys within a node. api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IRowMuta.title: Interface IRowMutator<K, V> | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IRowMuta.summary: Interface IRowMutator<K, V> Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Storage.dll Decides, per row, how a bulk insert applies an incoming row to a B+ tree. The bulk inserter calls Process(K, bool, in V, ref V, int) for each row to merge the incoming value with any existing value and choose the operation to perform, and GetSizePrefixSum(K[], ReadOnlySpan<int>, Span<int>) to estimate the serialized size of a run of rows for split planning. public interface IRowMutator<K, V> Type Parameters K The key type. V The value type. Methods GetSizePrefixSum(K[], ReadOnlySpan<int>, Span<int>) Accumulates, into sizes, the running prefix sum of the serialized byte size of the rows at the given indices. The bulk inserter uses this to decide where a page should be split. void GetSizePrefixSum(K[] keys, ReadOnlySpan<int> indices, Span<int> sizes) Parameters keys K[] The keys of the batch. indices ReadOnlySpan<int> The indices of the rows to size, in the order they will be inserted. sizes Span<int> The running per-row byte size totals to add to. Process(K, bool, in V, ref V, int) Processes a single incoming row and returns the operation to apply for it. When the key already exists the mutator can merge existingData into incomingData before the result is stored. GenericWriteOperation Process(K key, bool exists, in V existingData, ref V incomingData, int sortedIndex) Parameters key K The key of the incoming row. exists bool True when a value already exists for the key. existingData V The current stored value, when exists is true. incomingData V The incoming value, which may be modified in place, for example to merge it with the existing value. sortedIndex int Returns GenericWriteOperation The operation to apply for this row.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.html.title: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.html.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree Namespaces FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.Internal Classes BPlusTreeListComparer<K> BPlusTreeOptions<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> ListKeyContainer<K> ListValueContainer<V> PrimitiveListKeyContainer<T> PrimitiveListValueContainer<T> Structs BulkSearchKeyResult The result of searching one key within a leaf during a bulk search: which key it was and the index range it matched in that leaf. FindBoundriesResult The inclusive index range of the entries in a key container that match a searched key. When the key is not present both bounds are the bitwise complement of the index where it would be inserted. Interfaces IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> Applies a whole batch of rows to a B+ tree in a single pass. The batch is sorted by key and grouped by the leaf each row lands in, so each affected leaf is loaded once and any required node splits and merges are handled together. This is more efficient than upserting rows one at a time when writing a large batch. Each row's effect (insert, update, delete or no change) is decided by an IRowMutator<K, V>. IBPlusTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> A cursor over a B+ tree that enumerates the tree one leaf page at a time. Position it first with SeekFirst() or Seek(in K, in IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer>?), then enumerate it to walk the pages from that position onward; each yielded IBPlusTreePageIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> exposes the entries of a leaf. IBPlusTreeKeySerializer<K, TKeyContainer> Serializes and deserializes the keys of a B+ tree node, converting a node's key container to and from the bytes stored for that page. It also has checkpoint and initialize hooks for any serializer level metadata, such as schema information or global dictionaries. IBPlusTreePageIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> A view over a single leaf page yielded while iterating a B+ tree. It enumerates the page's key/value entries, exposes the page's Keys and Values containers, supports write locking, and can persist in-place edits through SavePage(bool). IBPlusTreeSerializerCheckpointContext IBPlusTreeSerializerInitializeContext IBPlusTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> A persistent B+ tree mapping keys to values. Keys and values are held in containers and the tree is paged through a state client, so it can grow beyond memory, offload to storage and be persisted across checkpoints. Reads and writes are asynchronous because a lookup may need to load pages from storage. IBplusTreeBulkSearch<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer, TComparer> Searches many keys against a B+ tree in a single pass. The keys are sorted and grouped by the leaf they fall in, so each leaf is loaded once and the results are produced one leaf at a time. After Start(K[], int), call MoveNextLeaf() in a loop and read CurrentLeaf and CurrentResults for each leaf until it returns false. A key whose match runs to the end of a leaf is carried over into the next leaf. Dispose the searcher to release the leaf page it holds. IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer> Defines how keys are compared and located within a key container for a B+ tree. Implementations can provide a search strategy tailored to how the keys are stored, for example a binary search over a sorted list. IBplusTreeSerializer<T> IBplusTreeValueSerializer<V, TValueContainer> Serializes and deserializes the values of a B+ tree node, converting a node's value container to and from the bytes stored for that page. It also has checkpoint and initialize hooks for any serializer level metadata, such as schema information or global dictionaries. IKeyContainer<K> Stores the keys of a single B+ tree node. Implementations choose how keys are laid out, for example a managed list or a contiguous unmanaged buffer, and the tree uses this abstraction to search, insert, update and remove keys within a node. Keys within a container are kept in sorted order. IRowMutator<K, V> Decides, per row, how a bulk insert applies an incoming row to a B+ tree. The bulk inserter calls Process(K, bool, in V, ref V, int) for each row to merge the incoming value with any existing value and choose the operation to perform, and GetSizePrefixSum(K[], ReadOnlySpan<int>, Span<int>) to estimate the serialized size of a run of rows for split planning. IValueContainer<V> Stores the values of a single B+ tree node, in the same order as the keys in the node's key container. Implementations choose how values are laid out, for example a managed list or a contiguous unmanaged buffer, and the tree uses this abstraction to read, insert, update and remove values within a node. Enums GenericWriteOperation The change a read-modify-write applied to a key. Delegates GenericWriteFunction<V> A read-modify-write callback invoked for a key during a write. It receives the value supplied to the write call, the current stored value (or default when absent), and whether a value currently exists, and returns the new value together with the GenericWriteOperation to apply.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.html.title: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Storage.html.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Storage Namespaces FlowtideDotNet.Storage.AppendTree FlowtideDotNet.Storage.AzureBlobs FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Comparers FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Exceptions FlowtideDotNet.Storage.FasterKV FlowtideDotNet.Storage.FileCache FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Memory FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Mimalloc FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Queue FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Serializers FlowtideDotNet.Storage.SqlServer FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateManager FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Utils Classes FileCacheOptions StateSerializeOptions Controls how state pages are serialized to the file cache and persistent storage, mainly how they are compressed. An instance left at its defaults applies no compression. Configure it through the storage builder, for example with ZstdPageCompression or NoCompression. StreamVersionInformation Identifies the version of a running stream so that persisted state can be matched to the plan that produced it. It is supplied through the stream builder (SetVersionInformation) and flows into storage initialization, where providers use it to detect plan changes between restarts and to scope stored data to a particular version. Structs SerializableObject A value handed to a file cache or persistent storage session to be written. It carries the data in one of two forms so callers do not have to serialize ahead of time: either a live ICacheObject together with the IStateSerializer that knows how to serialize it, or a block of bytes that has already been serialized. The bytes form is used when data is copied straight from the file cache to persistent storage, or for already serialized blobs such as metadata, where re-serialization is not needed. Interfaces IAppendTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer> A B+ tree that only supports appending entries in strictly increasing key order, which keeps all writes in the rightmost leaf and avoids the node splitting a general purpose tree needs. It is suited to time ordered data such as a time series, where new entries always have a larger key than everything already stored. Data is paged through a state client so the tree can grow beyond memory and be persisted across checkpoints. Enums CompressionMethod The granularity at which compression is applied to state pages. CompressionType The algorithm used to compress state pages before they are written to the file cache or persistent storage.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Sql.TablePr.title: Class TableProviderTableFunctionArguments | Flowtide.NET
api/FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Sql.TablePr.summary: Class TableProviderTableFunctionArguments Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Sql Assembly FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.dll Carries all SQL-parser context needed by an ITableProvider implementation to resolve a table-valued function call into a Substrait Relation. public class TableProviderTableFunctionArguments Inheritance object TableProviderTableFunctionArguments Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks An instance is created by the SQL visitor for every table-valued function reference it encounters — either as a standalone FROM source or as the right-hand side of a JOIN. When used in a JOIN, ParentRelation, JoinType, and JoinCondition are populated so the implementation can build a joined relation directly; all three are null when the function is used as a standalone FROM source. Implementations of TryHandleTableFunction(IReadOnlyList<string>, TableProviderTableFunctionArguments, out TableProviderTableFunctionResult?) receive this object and use Arguments, ExpressionVisitor, and EmitData to evaluate the individual SQL arguments and resolve column references. JoinType should be checked first when the function does not support being placed inside a JOIN. Properties Arguments Gets the raw SQL function arguments as parsed from the query. public Sequence<FunctionArg> Arguments { get; } Property Value Sequence<FunctionArg> Remarks Use ExpressionVisitor together with EmitData to convert each element to a Substrait Expression. The argument count and types are determined by the specific table function being implemented. EmitData Gets the column metadata that tracks the names, types, and ordinal positions visible in the current query scope at the point where the table function is referenced. public EmitData EmitData { get; } Property Value EmitData Remarks When the function appears as a standalone FROM source this is an empty EmitData instance. When called from a JOIN it contains the columns contributed by the left-hand ParentRelation, allowing argument expressions to reference columns from the left side of the join. ExpressionVisitor Gets the visitor used to convert raw SQL SqlParser.Ast.Expression nodes into Substrait ExpressionData values. public SqlExpressionVisitor ExpressionVisitor { get; } Property Value SqlExpressionVisitor Remarks Pass each argument expression together with EmitData to Visit(Expression, EmitData) to obtain the corresponding Substrait expression and its inferred type. JoinCondition Gets the raw SQL ON condition expression of the enclosing JOIN, or null when the function appears as a standalone FROM source or when no explicit ON clause was provided. public Expression? JoinCondition { get; } Property Value Expression Remarks Use ExpressionVisitor together with EmitData to convert this expression into a Substrait filter condition when building a joined relation. JoinType Gets the type of the enclosing SQL JOIN (such as INNER or LEFT OUTER), or null when the function appears as a standalone FROM source. public JoinType? JoinType { get; } Property Value JoinType? Remarks Implementations that do not support being placed inside a JOIN should check this property and throw when it is not null. ParentRelation Gets the left-hand Substrait Relation when this function is the right-hand side of a JOIN, or null when the function appears as a standalone FROM source. public Relation? ParentRelation { get; } Property Value Relation TableAlias Gets the optional alias declared for the table function in the SQL query, or null if no alias was specified. public string? TableAlias { get; } Property Value string Remarks For example, given FROM spicedb.func(...) AS f, this property returns "f". Column references qualified with the alias are resolved through EmitData.

api/FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.html.summary: Namespace FlowtideDotNet.Substrait Namespaces FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Conversion FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.CustomProtobuf FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Exceptions FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Expressions FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.FunctionExtensions FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Hints FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Relations FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Sql FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Type Classes Plan PlanModifier SubstraitDeserializer SubstraitSerializer Serializes a Plan into the Substrait protobuf format or its JSON representation, the counterpart of SubstraitDeserializer. A serialized plan is how plans travel between processes, for example to Orleans grains, and a deserialization always produces a fresh plan instance, which matters because building a stream mutates the plan in place.

Remarks This method is invoked synchronously within the checkpoint completion task. Implementations should avoid long-running or blocking operations; if coordination with an external system is required, consider starting a background task rather than blocking the checkpoint completion path. Access StreamName to identify the stream that completed the checkpoint. OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification) Called when the stream encounters an unhandled exception and is about to enter a failure state and initiate its automatic recovery sequence. public void OnFailure(StreamFailureNotification notification) Parameters notification StreamFailureNotification A StreamFailureNotification carrying the name of the stream that failed and the optional exception that caused the failure. Remarks This method is invoked synchronously within the failure transition. Implementations should avoid long-running or blocking operations, as this call occurs before the stream has stopped its running blocks. Access StreamName to identify the affected stream, and Exception for the cause of the failure, which may be null in cases where no specific exception was captured. WaitForCheckpoint(CancellationToken) public Task WaitForCheckpoint(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task

api/index.html.title: API Reference | Flowtide.NET
api/index.html.summary: API Reference This section contains the .NET API reference for Flowtide, generated from the XML documentation comments in the source code. Use the table of contents to browse the available namespaces and types, or the search box to find a specific member.

docs/connectors/catalogs.html.title: Catalogs | Flowtide.NET
docs/connectors/catalogs.html.summary: Catalogs It is possible to add connectors under catalogs, this can be useful if one wants to read data from data sources that may have the same logical name. Say two SQL Servers that have the same database names (or even schema and table names). Example reading data from a catalog when using SQL Server: ... FROM {catalogName}.{databaseName}.{schema}.{tableName} A catalog is added on the connector manager as follows: connectorManager.AddCatalog("catalogName", (connectors) => { // Add connectors here as normal }); It works the same with dependency injection: services.AddFlowtideStream("my_stream_name") ... .AddConnectors(connectorManager => { connectorManager.AddCatalog("catalogName", (connectors) => { // Add connectors here as normal }); });

docs/connectors/configurationdata.html.title: Configuration Data | Flowtide.NET
docs/connectors/configurationdata.html.summary: Configuration Data It is possible to use data from IConfiguration as a data source, this can be useful if one wants to use configuration inside the stream, for instance to filter out rows that do not exist in the configuration. Options Data Source The options data source uses IOptionsMonitor<TOptions> to listen to changes and to get the configuration settings. The Options data source is built-in as a default connector when installing FlowtideDotNet, so no extra nuget package is required. Options are added with the AddOptionsSource<TOptions> method on the connector manager. One of the main benefits of this connector comes if you have a configuration provider that allows reloading, then data can be updated without restarting the stream. Example: internal class TestOptions { public string? Name { get; set; } } var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args); builder.Services.AddOptions<TestConfig>() .Bind(builder.Configuration.GetSection("config")); builder.Services.AddFlowtideStream("test") .AddConnectors((connectorManager) => { connectorManager.AddOptionsSource<TestConfig>("config"); ... }) ... Configuration settings: { "config": { "name": "hello" } } SQL statement: INSERT INTO output SELECT name FROM config This stream would result in the following data: name hello Example using options data source as an exclude filter One scenario where this might be useful is to handle exclusions, say a specific row should not be sent to a destination. You could then have an options class similar to: internal class TestOptions { public List<string>? ExcludedIds { get; set; } } And an sql that looks the following: CREATE VIEW excluded_ids WITH (BUFFERED = true) AS select excludedId FROM config c INNER JOIN UNNEST(c.excludedIds) excludedId; INSERT INTO my_destination SELECT m.id, m.other FROM my_table m LEFT JOIN excluded_ids e ON m.id = e.excludedId WHERE e.excludedId is null; The buffered view helps with performance where it buffers the changes from the UNNEST statement so only changing rows are returned. We can then do a left join to match with the excluded ids but only return rows where there was no match.

docs/connectors/cosmosdb.html.title: CosmosDB Connector | Flowtide.NET
docs/connectors/cosmosdb.html.summary: CosmosDB Connector The CosmosDB connector allows you to insert data into a CosmosDB container. At this time only a sink is implemented, there is no support yet to have CosmosDB as a source. Sink The CosmosDB sink allows the insertion of data into a CosmosDB container. Note All CosmosDB insertions must contain a column called 'id' and also a column that matches the configured partition key on the CosmosDB container. Its implementation waits fully until the stream has reached a steady state at a time T until it writes data to the container. This means that its table output can always be traced back to a state from the source systems. To use the CosmosDB Sink add the following line to the IConnectorManager: connectorManager.AddCosmosDbSink("your regexp on table names", connectionString, databaseName, containerName); Example Having a column named 'id' and also a column matching the configured primary key is required for the sink to function. sqlBuilder.Sql(@" INSERT into cosmos SELECT userKey as id, companyId as pk, firstName, lastName FROM users "); connectorManager.AddCosmosDbSink("cosmos", connectionString, databaseName, containerName); ...

docs/connectors/customdata.html.title: Custom Data Source | Flowtide.NET
docs/connectors/customdata.html.summary: Custom Data Source It is possible to fetch data from a custom source such as an API, other database, file system, etc. There are multiple ways: Implement GenericDataSource(Async) - Simplified source which should return C# objects. This is the recomended way to start implementing a source. Implement ReadBaseOperator - This allows the creation of a low-level read operator, where serialization, state storage, watermarks and checkpointing must be handled. Generic Data Source The generic data source allows easy implementation against custom sources that returns C# objects. It allows: Full batch reloads, where all the data is imported again and delta is computed. Delta loads, where delta should be returned. Custom watermark provided by the source. Scheduling of full batch and delta reloads. There are two classes that can be implemented for the generic data source: GenericDataSourceAsync - Data is returned by an IAsyncEnumerable, this should be used with remote sources. GenericDataSource - Data is returned by an IEnumerable, which should be used in cases where data is already in memory. When implementing a generic data source, it is important to think about memory usage, for instance, do not fetch all rows and store them in memory and then return them, this can cause huge memory spikes or out of memory. Instead yield return values and fetch the data in batches. The operator stores the data in B+ trees that will be temporarily stored on disk if the memory usage is too high. Implementation example public class ExampleDataSource : GenericDataSourceAsync<User> { private readonly IUserRepository _userRepository; public ExampleDataSource(IUserRepository userRepository) { _userRepository = userRepository; } // Fetch delta every 1 second public override TimeSpan? DeltaLoadInterval => TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1); // Reload all data every 1 hours, this is not required, but can be useful. // If for instance deletes cant be found in deltas from the source, // a full reload would find all deleted rows. public override TimeSpan? FullLoadInterval => TimeSpan.FromHours(1); protected override IEnumerable<FlowtideGenericObject<User>> DeltaLoadAsync(long lastWatermark) { var changes = _userRepository.GetChangesFromWatermarkAsync(lastWatermark); await foreach(var change in changes) { yield return new FlowtideGenericObject<User>(change.Id, change, change.Timestamp); } } protected override IEnumerable<FlowtideGenericObject<User>> FullLoadAsync() { var data = _userRepository.GetAllDataAsync(lastWatermark); await foreach(var row in data) { yield return new FlowtideGenericObject<User>(row.Id, row, row.Timestamp); } } } To use your data source, add the following to the ConnectorManager: connectorManager.AddCustomSource( "{the table name}", (readRelation) => new ExampleDataSource(userRepository)); All rows must return an identifier that is used when calculating changes of the data. This key can be accessed when querying with the special name of __key. Push-based delta loading Instead of polling with DeltaLoadAsync, it is possible to use a push-based model where the source controls when data is submitted. This can be useful for sources such as message queues, webhooks or event streams. To use push-based loading, override IsPushBased to return true and implement RunDeltaLoad: public class EventDrivenSource : GenericDataSourceAsync<User> { private readonly Channel<User> _channel = Channel.CreateUnbounded<User>(); public override bool IsPushBased => true; // Set to null to disable interval-based triggering public override TimeSpan? DeltaLoadInterval => null; public override async Task RunDeltaLoad( IDeltaLoadContext<User> context, CancellationToken cancellationToken) { while (await _channel.Reader.WaitToReadAsync(cancellationToken)) { // Begin a transaction, this acquires the checkpoint lock // ensuring all items in this batch are included in the same checkpoint. await using var tx = await context.BeginTransactionAsync(cancellationToken); while (_channel.Reader.TryRead(out var user)) { var obj = new FlowtideGenericObject<User>( user.Id, user, DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds(), false); await tx.SubmitAsync(obj, cancellationToken); } // Disposing the transaction releases the checkpoint lock } } public override IAsyncEnumerable<FlowtideGenericObject<User>> FullLoadAsync() { // Return initial data... } } RunDeltaLoad is a long-running method that controls its own loop, instead of returning an enumerable per interval. Data is submitted through transactions which manage the checkpoint lock, ensuring all items in one transaction end up in the same checkpoint. Trigger data reloads programatically The generic data source also registers triggers that allows the user to notify the stream when a reload should happen. The following triggers are registered: full_load - Does a full load on all running generic data sources delta_load - Does a delta load on all running generic data sources full_load_{tableName} - Full load for a specific source delta_load_{tableName} - Delta load for a specific source Example on calling a trigger: await stream.CallTrigger("delta_load", default); Calling the triggers programatically can be useful if having an interval would cause too much latency for the data. SQL Table Provider There is a table provider as well for the generic data source, that can be used to easily import table metadata from a class. Example: sqlPlanBuilder.AddGenericDataTable<User>("users"); If you are starting Flowtide with dependency injection, a table provider is added automatically, so this step is not required. Generic data sink The generic data sink allow the implementation of a sink that reads the rows as C# classes. This limits the stream to only send those specific columns, but it can be useful im cases such as API integrations where there is a strict schema. Implementation example Create a class that inherits from GenericDataSink. internal class TestDataSink : GenericDataSink<User> { public override Task<List<string>> GetPrimaryKeyNames() { return Task.FromResult(new List<string> { "{primaryKeyColumnName}" }); } public override async Task OnChanges(IAsyncEnumerable<FlowtideGenericWriteObject<User>> changes, Watermark watermark, bool isInitialData, CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await foreach(var userChange in changes) { if (!userChange.IsDeleted) { // Do upsert to destination } else { // Do delete against destination } } } } Add the generic data sink to the ConnectorManager: connectorManager.AddCustomSink("{tableName}", (rel) => new testDataSink()); Fetching existing data On initial startup, fetching existing data from the destination is optional. If you do not override this method, Flowtide assumes the destination is empty and simply sends all current stream data to OnChanges as initial inserts. However, if you want Flowtide to calculate a delta based on the current state of the destination system (e.g., to only push changes, and to emit delete operations for any rows in the destination that are no longer in the incoming stream), you can override the GetExistingData method: public override IAsyncEnumerable<User> GetExistingData() { // Fetch all existing users from the destination return _userRepository.GetAllUsersAsync(); } If you override this method: During initial load, Flowtide compares the existing destination data with the incoming stream data. It will automatically trigger insert/update operations for new or changed data, and delete operations for any rows in the destination that are no longer present in the incoming stream data. Note GetExistingData is only executed when the stream starts up for the first time. Once the initial synchronization is complete and the first checkpoint has been committed, it will not be called again (even on subsequent stream restarts). From that point onward, the sink relies entirely on Flowtide's internally persisted state to calculate subsequent changes. Custom sink sample A runnable console application demonstrating a custom sink and source is available under samples/CustomSinkSample. It shows how initial sink synchronization with GetExistingData works and how the sink handles data updates. Adding custom converters It is possible to register custom converters to convert .NET objects into the column format, and back.
